content: IMHO the premium is worth the cost. I love how user friendly this app is and how much information it gives you. The barcode scanner and the ability to import recipes makes it easy to track. This app keeps up with all of the nutrients you eat each day making it easier to hit my goals. Out of all the calorie tracking apps I've tried, this one is the best. A lot of the other apps are moving to AI for tracking but I don't find that accurate and hate using it. So glad MFP doesn't use it.

So far so good. only used it a couple days but it tracts nutrition and gives you the protein, fat, and carbs that I was looking for. I love that tracking over meals, days, and hopefully in total. I am interested in whether I can download this information for later use. The app pumps you to use their paid version which allows you to scan barcodes, barcodes would be nice but trying to eat less processed foods.

I started using this app to cut some weight down and have continued after. It's very easy to use even with having a subscription option. It's still great for non spenders! Of course, some foods aren't logged correctly, but they let you log your own items in that situation. Also, the nutrition goals are averaged, so you can go over them pretty quickly. Depending on your activity levels, you can adjust it yourself instead to match your lifestyle.

They show the oldest weight first now?!? I don't care to scroll from 2013 down to 2025 to see current weights. I select the "Week" time frame and it only shows todays weight with a straight line in the graph. I select "1 Month" and it still shows all the way back to 2013. I keep having to log in. Please fix this. I love the app, but this is frustrating. Update: Issue has been fixed and I am back to loving the app I have been so loyal to for years! Thank you~

This makes it much easier to log my meals for the day! like how you can add certain meals that you make/eat so that you don't have to calculate the calories every time. Previously I was just writing them down in a journal. I still log it in a journal for therapeutic reasons but this helps me to be able to log something right away so if I do write it down, I can go back to it to remember exactly what I ate. also being able to log workouts is very helpful too!

Easy to use! I like that it sends gentle reminders. There are a lot of foods already listed in its database and the being able to snap a picture of the barcode and having it list everything is nice. My only gripe really is when creating a recipe you have to know roughly how many servings you are making and how much of each item. I don't usually measure.. I throw stuff in. I do highly suggest MyFitnessPal if you want to get serious about tracking things about your diet! It's very user friendly

Great Fitness Tracking App! MyFitnessPal has just gotten better and better over time. The food tracking function is the best I've seen, it has all the foods & will figure out calories and macros from your own recipes. It can communicate with my galaxy watch and fit bit to keep all my data consolidated and together. The volume of workouts, fitness coaching and community is also excellent. I upgraded, but used the free version for years because its so feature packed. Highly recommend.

Best $80 I have spent for physical health. I am still learning the convenience of logging in food that got better with updates since 2013. MFP premium is the greatest app I have ever used when it comes to looking at personal statistics on a daily intake of nutrition. I love it so much that I fell off my health journey and have come back to it due to it's essential use in my daily regimen. People complain about macro-counting to be a chore while I find it fairly easy with this app.

Just brilliant. So comprehensive, a massive database of foods and exercises. The free version is one of the best apps I've ever used, let alone the pay-for version. By tracking meals and snacks using this app, I dropped 10 lbs this summer and have kept it off, even through Halloween candy season.

I love(d) using MyFitnessPal to log daily calories and exercise. I even lost nearly 40 pounds. The new format is busy and messy making it harder to find things that were easily on the previous home screen. There are items I never use and I have to scroll down through a bunch of sections to get to exercise. And dashboard (Today) is no longer customizable. I prefer the charts/visual layout of the previous version. UPDATE: they listened to us and returned to previous layout! thank you!

Latest update 20.3.0 broke ordering of entries. Used to move to bottom of list when revised, which is super helpful when organizing workouts, but they do not move now. Please revert back to previous functionality, as it always been. I love this app! It keeps me on track and honest about my caloric intake. I'm amazed at the huge database content, it's got just about everything in the grocery store and so many restaurants.

Update: the features that made this app AMAZING are no longer free. I understand, but due to that, I no longer use this. Love how I can create my own recipes and it calculates the calories, protein, carbs and types of fats per serving. Although I wish there was some way of editing those recipes in case I find good substitutions or change serving sizes. The scanning option is soooooo convenient for food items!!!!! Overall, best app for keeping track of my food intake

Update: Since MFP's changes to move formerly standard features behind the premium paywall, I've heard from friends that it's less good and doesn't integrate with other apps until you pay. So this review is for the premium version. YMMV Really enjoy this health tracker. Mostly use it for tracking calories. Lots of foods in its list, which makes nutrition easy to track. Lists many restaurant menus as well, so I don't need to go to their websites.

Good app, would be better if some things were free and not needing premium. It's kind of difficult to find the food I ate, considering needing more options, but that's not a big deal. But when I can't find the food, I have to 'make one', and it gets annoying when it asks me for more information that is clearly optional. Otherwise, good app to keep track of myself.

I was under the impression I pay 20 dollars a month for no ads and yet I get ads saying to go premium. However I'm getting the same stuff I got when I had the free trial of the pay version. Other than that I don't have any problems. It's accurate and easy to navigate. I haven't tried the groups yet but the food diary and the macros are stellar. Will re rate 5 * when I can get an answer if my version is paid or not. EDIT I read further on down and it seems there is a glitch being worked on. 5*!

Just writing a review so it stops asking me. it's actually a good app. I haven't had any pesky ads, the pop-up only appears if you click something that requires $$. Searching for food is very easy, and it tells you if it's a certified food they logged. You can adjust portions to all sorts of measurements. It saves all your logs, so you can easily scroll though and just quick add your most common foods. So far, not disappointed and have been able to look at my eating habits differently

I love this app and use it daily to track macros. It has a lot of great features such as customized meals and a huge selection of healthy recipes. It is great for accountability. This app has changed what I put into my body because you can set limits for carb, protein, and fat intake. My problem area was unhealthy snacks. If you sign up for a free trial, make sure it is the one you want. It was a hassle for me to get reimbursed for a yearly subscription.

Used to be a free user, happy to pay for it now. Years ago, as a free customer I lost 25 pounds in a year and it never felt like a diet. This app keeps simply keeps a running total of your calorie consumption so you know where your empty calories are hiding. Eat, scan a bar code, repeat. Connect up a step tracker and you earn a bit more. turns out I had too many calories in something I eat every day. Only a week of usage and I've identified the problem. Happy to pay since it works so well

Used this off and on for a while. For tracking nutrition and calories, this is the best bang for your buck there is. A few advertisements for a quality tracking program is a pretty good deal. You still have to take some of the nutrition information with a grain of salt since most of them are crowd-sourced, but I'm just trying to estimate to begin with to better inform nutrition choices, so getting down to the exact calorie isn't vital. I like it!

Calorie counting has never been easier. Gone are the pieces of paper with scratched out lines. This app makes history-keeping, tracking, and even math super easy. Love the option to scan bar codes, track water, and exercise. I didn't even mention the comprehensive food database and ability to enter recipes for calorie information. 👍

This app allowed me to give up Weight Watchers and save me $20+ a month. Does all I need it to do. I'm able to tweak some features (calorie allotment, but not that much, and ratio of carbs/fat/protein), and it's great when I can see at the end of the day unused calories left over. Can import recipes & sync to my Fitbit. I'm not an intense athlete so some people are grumbling about some of the features that were once free but now you need a premium membership. But for me it's great!

This application is great for tracking calorie intake. The food database is comprehensive and usually accurate. No application is perfect, but it's hard to find things wrong with this one. Even so, a way to categorize and organize recipes would be nice for people who cook on a regular basis. Not everyone meal preps the same old things.

Useful app. Free version does everything you need, helps you track calories, macronutrients, micronutrients, keeps track of your weight. The only thing I cant figure out is how they logged the crack rock they must be smoking if they think anyone's going to pay $20 a month for a premium membership that does little more than it's basic membership.

Really great resource to help with your healthy lifestyle goals! Love how you can scan barcodes from food packages or search for other items without a barcode. However, this app gives you do much more than just food tracking! Recipes, Workouts, all kinds of cool extras. I did sign up for the premium app, but it's so work it to me! I paid for the 1 year option. Please keep up the high level of quality!

Update: Over the years, I have tried so many fitness apps, some free and some quite expensive, but I always compare them to MyFitnessPal. They just don't come close. I love how sensitive the scanner is, how easy it is to search and log my foods, and how it has evolved over time to include plans and articles to read. In my opinion, this is the best fitness app out there! It has helped me to lose 20lbs!

My Fitness Pal (premium)is the best app I have found! It's very easy to scan food items and enter them into my diary, see my calories, macros, and nutrients, keep track of my water, and keep track of my exercises. The articles are interesting and very informative. I recommend this app for everyone who wants to keep track of their food, water, and exercises. Excellent app!

Great tool! Helps you easily watch intake of protein, calories, carb, and fat. Being able to set caloric intake and macro ratio goals and then navigate between the food diary, nutrition, and macros allows one to make better choices while still eating a variety of foods. Wish it interfaced with more of the less expensive fitness devices.

Great app! Its easy to use and very intuitive. It even remembers commonly eaten foods and the usual quantity you've entered in the past. One critique is that the calorie information is not very reliable. Unless it has been verified (which is indicated by a green check mark) you really need to look it up and double-check. My biggest complaint, however, is the price of the unlimited version!! Many apps are purchased with a one time fee. My Fitness Pal is ONE HUNDRED DOLLARS PER YEAR. Ridiculous!!

I love how everything you can imagine is in here ... I have yet to stump the database. It talks to Map My Walk too, so it adjusts your calories for the day. I like that you can even plug in the ingredients for your own recipes, too, so you can figure out nutrition info for them. Haven't found any bugs in the program yet either. UPDATE: I have found a couple of things that stump the program, but they're products from other countries with non-English labeling, so can't fault the app!

Between this and my Fitbit Versa, I have a great way of tracking my progress, nutrition, daily activity, etc. The interdace is intuitive and easy to use. The food database is great, including some of my favorite vegan restaurants and snacks. Wish there was a vegan filter for the recipe feed, but otherwise it's a great tool.

I've used this app for years to track my food intake, the free version. Allows me to closely track my calories, and watch my overall carbs, proteins, and fats which is perfect for me! It has a lot of the foods I eat in here to access them and add them easily. The recipe import process is super easy also! The only downside is you cannot modify or review the ingredients on the app, but you can on the full version online.

Just began using this app again. I am so pleased! The food entries have evolved to perfection!! The data base has expanded! The nutrient data is accurate! Vitamin supplements are there! I used to spend a lot of time checking the accuracy and reporting the errors, along with making new, accurate entries for my foods. It was tedious and time consuming, but I knew it was worth it. Thanks everyone for adding to the data base! Phenomenal. I'd give it 10 stars. ✨

I have had an easy time tracking my calories with the app. I have tried keeping track my own but this is much easier. I love how the app brakes down what my other goals for the day should be. (Ex. Fat, sugar, sodium...ect.) Being able to scan the barcode is a nice feature so that your not scrolling trying to find the perfect match. After you click that you have completed logging your food for the day, the app tells you what you could weigh in 5 weeks if every day resembles that one. Motivation!

I have had no problems at all with this app. I don't even use all of the features and when I find something new, such as how many carbs, protein and fats you've eaten for the day, I'm even loving this app more. It even allows you to enter a recipe and it calculates the calories per serving for you.. how great is that?! I found that it allows you to enter your water intake and it also syncs with different exercise apps and adjusts your calorie requirements according. This app offers so much!

I only have the free version. And does everything needed. My only goal is to simply track my calories to ensure I stay on track for the day. I don't get into counting macros/ tracking carbs or anything that complicated. So I'm not too sure how those features work. But if you are a beginner or just looking to be more mindful about calorie consumption. Works perfectly Also, syncs great to Google Fit, so that's cool as well. 🙂

I'm using the full paid version of the app combined (and under the advisement) of a nutritionist. I've found that the process feels a bit daunting at first (definitely not insurmountable, but not "an automatic breeze) however I tried coming home from the grocery store and scanning everything in as I put it away, now I had it all stored automatically to call up at any given meal.. excellent! Once I was in a routine, I noticed it's very help, accurate and a big part of me reaching my goals!

*Update: the MyFitnessPal team was aware of these issues and took the steps to correct the issues and keep people updated. I have changed my review because the issues I had were fixed in a timely manner and the team took the time to post updates. Original: App became completely buggy. For whatever reason the app started messing up yesterday and I couldn't add my recipes online or on the app. So I tried again today, and I couldn't even log my meals. So I uninstalled, tried clearing cache and data, reinstalled, logged out and back in, etc. (not necessarily in this order). Now when I log in, none of my online is attached aside from my username and starting weight. Meals, recipes and history, all gone. They appear if I log in online, but not the app.

*Update: I was just able to log in again! Thank you for the prompt response and for your efforts to fix the issue! Great customer service! 5 stars! 😊😊😊 (I've been enjoying this app overall, and I've lost at least 7 pounds since starting to count my calories the beginning of January! However, today all that's changed. The app will not load. I uninstalled and installed it, and it STILL is not loading. Please fix so I can give 5 stars!!! Thank you!)

Only app I have ever paid for premium options, and it was worth every penny. I track carbs, fats, proteins, and calories intake. It allows me to keep a detailed log of my workouts and subtracts the estimated amount of calories burned from my total consumed from the day, if I want it to. So versatile. I can do quick adds of foods not listed, my own meals, and quickly find my frequently eaten foods. And, you can add friends and comment on each other's progress for added support! I love this app and I have already, and will continue to recommend it to anyone else that wants help with their fitness. The only suggestion I would make is to be able to track vitamins B and D, but that's just me being super picky. Lol

I used this app 5 years ago and lost 50 pounds. Sadly I gained 40 of it back. I can't recommend this app enough. It keeps you focused and on track. It's even better than the last time I used it especially for tracking food. Now you can scan the package and you have all the nutritional information. How cool is that? It also syncs with my Garmin bracelet to include my activities. Week one and I've lost 4 lbs! Keep up the great work!

Upgraded to My Fitness Pal Premium to let the app do the work of computing the macros. It's been awesome. Not only in tracking but giving you all the data needed to make appropriate nutritional decisions instantly with just a few clicks. Have not had any problems and the learning curve is simply intuitive. Try it free for 30 days!

Once you get used to it, this app is very user friendly. I got a new Fitbit, and I opted to link myfitnesspal to the Fitbit app instead of logging it all there. The interface of myfitnesspal is easier, and there are more food options from what I could tell. I love that I can create meals and recipes! Not only does it help me track more accurately, but I can reference the recipes to make them again!

5 years ago I lost 40lbs using this app. At the same time I quit smoking....doctor couldn't believe it! Got lazy over the years and gained some of my weight back. Starting using the app again January 1 and have lost 10lbs already! The app holds you accountable and forces you to make good choices. Once you get a week or two in and start to see the results, it becomes a good habit!

Updating my review. Don't know if it was just a glitch with the update (lost my ability to view and track macros) but my macros tracker is back up and running. Definitely recommend this app to everyone. It has one of the best food databases out there and literally makes it easy. 100% improvement from any other app I've ever used and 100% easier than pen and paper. With the power this app unleashes the only thing missing is your dedication. Get tracking, get moving.

1. Great food/calorie tracker, comprehensive database; 2. Syncs with most fitness tracking devices and apps; 3. Now syncs with SHealth!! Bought premium once that happened; 4. Still gives 0 calorie credit for weightlifting, so no matter how much time you put in at the gym, only the cardio affects your calories--unless you put the weights into the other fitness tracker, but MFP is a little more comprehensive about specific exercise tracking. Depends what you're looking for.

I use the free version to follow the ketogenic diet. So far it works great. It even feeds the calorie and exercise info to my Fitbit so I can chart my caloric burn along with my heart rate. I love the ease of entering multiple food items and the vast number of foods already loaded, but the barcode scanner makes it very easy to add new ones as well.

Great app. Great articles and recipes. Amazing database with the ability to scan foods. Only thing they need to improve is the goals tab, which only give you one number for blood pressure, and could include more measurable variables, like resting heart rate, or hip, or cholesterol. A personal coaching option would also be nice.

I had used the Lose It app RELIGIOUSLY before this, and was frustrated that you weren't able to edit homemade recipes that you entered (say, if you made it with beef one time, and maybe just had chicken on hand for the next time you made it). It sounds like a little detail, but for someone who cooks a lot, it was a real pain. So glad you can do that and more with this app!1

By far the best and easiest nutrition tracker I have ever used. The bar code scanner makes tracking ridiculously easy. I have yet to find a food that isn't in the data base (even 5 guys bunless bacon cheese burger). Links with my samsung watch so tracking exercise is super easy too. I have just started using the premium app and so far it is worth the money.

VERY user friendly. Especially love the fact that you can scan in your food labels and simply type in or select portion size. You can also add your own recipes and save for future selection so you don't have to itemize every time. The end of day nutritional summary is amazing and has made me realize how many empty calories I was consuming, and how much sugar I was eating!! I'm learning more and more every day how to eat healthier!

This Can help you get your food house in order. I like the built in scanner to easily log harder to find things in the data base such as wine or whiskey. I used to think i was staying in my 2000 calorie zone but now i know exactly where I'm at. I can also log certain exercises into it to account for those additional allotted calories. Although that is limited to strength training generic or a set cardiovascular constant workout. That calculation could use some improvement to its data.

Easy to use to track food consumption. Unlike the Diabetes app I used to use, the food lookup and barcode scanner aren't neutered. So far I've found each food I need to look up. It works with Under Armor Record to adjust for calories burned during workouts, and keeps me on track with gentle warnings when I'm getting too close to a fat, or sugar limit.

Best app for counting macros!! You do have to pay to gain access to marco counting but it is worth it. I have yet to find a barcode the scanner does not recognize and I buy plenty of international and off brand products. I have a smart watch that syncs cardio activity but have to do a little research on your own for weight training.

I love this app. I have been using it for years. It's easy to use and keeps getting better with updates and features. Since there is such a large community, the food database keeps growing and makes it easier to accurately log food. It syncs with various apps and devices so it's awesome that I can have everything all in one place. One of my favorite features is being able to create my own recipes and use them when logging my meals. Being able to tailor the macros is also a huge bonus!

I haven't gotten the pro version yet, i wanted to test out the regular app first. I've been using it for 10 days. So far I absolutely LOVE this app. for a busy sahm, this is a life saver to help keep me on track for my weight loss goals. I love that almost everything i need to put into my meals i can find by searching for it. Also the bar code scanner is AMAZING. So far im down 9 pounds in 10 days. this is going to be my go to app to help me finally lose that baby weight, best app ever!!!!

A lot of the time, the app is really useful and functional. However, it does get buggy from time-to-time and won't update correctly, won't open, or won't enter food correctly. This is rare. I wish MFP was more diligent about editing enteries for accuracy. Lots of foods in the database are old or simply inaccurate.

The app got even better than it was 8 years ago. Still doing the free version, hate ads so debating on premium, maybe. I've never been able to keep with logging my foods until now. Things I thought were healthy weren't and I've made better food choices because of this app. I've made it apart of my daily routine and am sticking with it this time around. Love all the new feed of healthy stuff to read about now too!

I'm just getting started, but having never counted calories before, I'm finding this app surprisingly helpful and easy to use. I was away without internet or data over the weekend and really missed it, but I was able to log my food and exercise when I got home. It's making me very conscious of my food choices, and I'm becoming much more selective about what I "spend" my calorie intake on.

I'm giving this app 5 stars because I lost over 30 pounds using it. I loved how easy it was to track my food, that it worked with my samsung watch, and that it projected your weight 5 weeks out if everyday was like this day. It really motivated me to keep eating healthy, because the results WILL come. Now that they started charging for the scanner, I no longer need this app though. I can manually add food straight into my samsung app. Thank you for all you help fitnesspal! You helped me a lot!

I love the accountability this app provides. I especially look forward to logging in activities & getting credit for that. Knowing I have to journal whatever food I put in my mouth helps me to be more disciplined in deciding if the calories are worth it. The app won't provide a daily summary if my calorie consumption is too low so it also discourages under eating. I love all the features & that it's free!

Excellent App!! So easy to use that I have been usintg it for 6+ years!!! Love how you track Fitness and Food to help maintain or lose weight. Scanning barcodes is my favorite feature. Challenges can be joined to help motivate both fitness and food choices. Groups of friends can also use this app as a support system for accountability. Goals can be set to keep you in check daily.

Great for tracking food calories!!!! And it's quick and easy to input food. I love being able to scan a food's barcode if it's not already listed in the app. To track fitness calories, it was not as easy. I take classes like yoga sculpt, barre, and pilates and these were not options so I had to guess. If you do more standard cardio like running, bike, or swimming then you'd be better able to track calories burned because those options are in the app.

I've used this app over the years and it helps me chart the progress I've made. It interfaces with things like my Fitbit and other apps like Runkeeper to have everything in one spot. Most foods are surprisingly already in the database, down to the brand name. It's easy to add foods or even combine foods into your favorite meals for easier data entry. I can even set target macros for the keto WOE I'm doing now and that helps me plan my meals. Love what a free app can do.

I like the ease of use... plenty of options to select foods from a variety of categories...also ability to scan purchase code to get nutrition information. That's helped me a lot. I love that the app alerts me to log meals. I'm pleased and look fwd to achieving my weight goals based on calorie counting goal per day. seeing it daily motivates me to follow through as well as make wiser calculated foos choices...It's low maintenance and high impact (which is what I needed)

Love this app! Easy to use and helps you with calorie counting. I use the premium version and I find it worth every penny. I use it mainly for carb counting. MFP's food database is really good. I have used other calorie counting apps before and have had to manually enter some of the foods I eat. MFP is great in that I rarely run into that issue. You just need to be good.about logging in your food every day. You ca set reminders to help you do so.

This app is the best of the best! It is so easy to use - just scan in the upc's of the food or beverage you had and the app automatically charts your daily nutrition! I am still learning about all the features. Exercise and activity is tracked so you can see what is still needed, as is your water intake. It is an important tool that helps me manage carbs and diabetes. I recommend it to everyone!

It has a wide variety of exercises and foods to help you log easily. It helps track water, macros, and nutrients which is really helpful in knowing what you are getting too much/little of. It's by far the easiest app to navigate in this field. I have lost 14 lbs in about a month using this app as well as lowering my carb intake and exercising.

So far, so good. I use it for the calorie tracking. Which has been the most helpful to me. The other aspects are ok. It's social aspects are also lacking, but I'm actually fine with that. As the calorie tracking is my primary use. Adding ones exercises is lacking, as I use machines only at my gym and this app doesn't support enough machine options for me. So yeah, great calorie tracking, only ok on everything else. Still love it.

I have been using this app about a well and so far I like it. It did not get 5 stars because I feel the calories burned during exercise is higher than it should be. Also, out would be nice if water could be tracked by the ounce since that its usually how liquids are measured. Update: I restarted using this app. Many improvements including tracking water by the ounce and importing recipes from a website to track meal calories. I'm thrilled!

I have changed my way of eating & this app is a necessary part of keeping track. Since starting I have lost 60 lbs and have gone into remission from active MCTD. This app has an amazing database of foods and tells me my carb/calorie/fat content so that I can monitor my way of eating. It allows you to save repeat foods/meals without having to re-enter individual ingredients. The recipe feature is very helpful. Enter ingredients & total savings & you can enter it into your diary.

I really like mfp but not enough to pay the price of premiem. There are other apps out there that scan barcodes &/or enter foods & nutrician manually for free. It just doesn't offer anything that special to pay $75 for premium, for me. But for those that can afford it & will use it for a year, it is helpful letting you know where you stand at reaching your goal for weight loss.

This app has come a long way. Now not only can I scan or find foods easily, but when uploading a recipe say from a book or your own you can now search online and if you find a recipe that's close enough use that one cause it will let you scan the items you find use during edit. Which I have to say it's a super neat feature. I'm glad there are a lot of new features that I can use even with paying the premium.

I'm rather new, but I have been trying to navigate many apps to see what I like and what works. I find a simple bit fit and myfitness pal to be the best combination for success. if you are counting calories, you should log, and this is where the program really shines. If you are new as well, I suggest go straight to Fitbit and MFP and avoid decission fatigue. It's just enough to keep you focused on the prize.

I really enjoy using this application! It's very convenient and thorough. They have complete info on nearly every food or restaurant I've ever input. The scan feature allows you to quickly log any commercial product by it's barcode. It can link with my Fitbit and gives percentages of all nutrients logged so if you have a protein, carb, fat, sodium goal, you can easily track it. Although there is a premium fee-based version available, this free one more than meets my needs.

I had heard about this app and now use it and LOVE it. It keeps me accountable, and it's like a psychological game with yourself in the sense that you want to do better in the areas you see you could do better, like striving to exceed the number of steps from the day prior. I also LOVE that I can sync it with my Garmin fitness tracker. Superb!! 5 stars from me!!

This app has been exceedingly helpful, for me. My weight had caused some health problems, and step 1 was getting weight off. The scanning option makes life so easy ! Between this app, and a simple food scale, im down 30 lbs, in 5 months. I input recipes we use frequently, so i can grab them easily, the next time. Being able to see everything, under the nutrition portion, really helps me make food decisions, based on what i have had already, for the day. Dont like that premium is subscription

I've tried several calorie counting, weight management apps and this is the only one I've used that allows me to print out my daily intake diary to show my doctor. I am still exploring all that is available via this app. Also, I want to mention that just about every food I enter in my food diary, the nutritional components are already listed which means I don't have to take the time to do that. Someone has already eaten that item and written it down. Yay.

Best free food tracker app available IMO. Love this app, it keeps me accountable to my food intake. It's very intuitive and even offers a macro breakdown of what you're eating. Offers helpful suggestions and content (so appreciated) to keep me motivated and on target. Would recommend to anyone making a lifestyle change with regard to fitness and diet.

I don't have the premium version, but the app used regularly is great for keeping track of calories lost and gained based on exercise and consumption of food. I never not find a food on the calorie menu so it's very easy and the app reminds you when you forget to log the food you eat. Great for weight loss, maintenance and gain.

Life changing app! Highly recommended. I started off 205 at my highest. Now I'm around 165 and feel great. One day I just decided to dedicate myself to logging in my food in this app daily . The key is staying consistent and staying as close as possible to caloric deficit as possible. I also managed to fit in healthy ice cream healthy pizza and still see results. One thing that helped alot as well was exercising on top of it all 1-3x a week. This app is a great starting point. Good luck to all!

I'm a yo-yo dieter. I have a hard time sticking to diets. One thing that has always worked, no matter what diet I was following, was MyFitnessPal. It is so easy to use. I can scan most of the foods I eat and it adds everything for me. If it can't be scanned, it is probably listed in their database and a quick search pulls it up. I tend to overeat and this app really helps me stay mindful of how many empty calories I consume a day.

Helps keep me accountable and it's a great way to have a goal and a guide to stick to it. Has a food list history search for common foods you'll eat. It counts calories for you and has a whole dictionary of meals from restaurants which is extremely helpful -EXAMPLE- the Rock didnt have a nutrition menu so I wasnt sure how to calculate my meal so I entered the name and it had it on the list.

I purchased the premium service several times over several years. In summary, logging food is one of the best ways to accurately track calories and spot trends. Unfortunately, it was hitting over 6 tracking sites in several domains due to Ads on the non-premium version. A few end in .xyz. I worked with the Devs and the issue is resolved.

Just by counting calories has made me aware of how much I actually eat. Losing weight by trying to stay near my allowance. Database has found everything I've scanned. I wish that the servings would be more consistent. Some solid food is measured by volume and some by mass. Solid food should be listed by mass and liquids by volume. This would make calculating serving size easier. 5/31/20 I'm still using this app to count calories and workouts. I've been using it for 6 years and have kept my down

absolutely love this app, I use it to log all my food through out the day. Its easy to find foods or scan them in. It remembers what I eat most so I can just select them and go. It tells me the kinds of nutrients I'm getting, things I'm eating too much of, and what my foods are high in. It let's me post pictures of my weight progress. And my friend has the app too so we can follow each other which helps motivate me. I even pay for premium which I very, very rarely do on apps.

Good, simple way to track calories, carbs, protein, fat, fiber, and calcium. Some items in database are misspelled, and some have incorrect values, so you have to keep an eye on them, but overall really pleased. Love the scan function for foods! Had to add this, though, as an update, if you have to reinstall it due to glitches in the goals (like 3,500,000 mg needed for daily Na intake), don't expect to get help quickly when you can't log in ( even after 2 pw resets). Might have to wait.....

Love it!!! So far, I have been able to add everything I eat, it is so simple and they have had everything I was looking for! I did buy a store brand item today and it wasn't in the app, but I discovered, you can scan barcodes to add them into your diary!! I've lost 5 lbs in 10 days! And I haven't even changed my eating habits yet, that is counting calories alone. So I am still eating what I want, just not eating a ton of it.

exceptional. They seem to have all info from restaurants and pretty much every brand of food you can buy in the store for an accurate reading on your calories. I'm only disappointed because I am pregnant and there is no setting for pregnancy. When it comes to doing workout videos I was disappointed that there is no list of calorie loss for beach body vids, etc.

Hands down the best app out there to motivate. Calorie counter, they have nearly millions of food options including foods that you can add, different outcomes, challenges, the community, reminders. There is no reason to NOT have this app if you're trying to be healthy. Can't express how good this is, thank you.

Very helpful app and quite easy to use. With this app, I've lost so far 6 pounds so far in the week I've used it, combined with a good diet and exercise. It has an extensive list of foods to help keep track of calories and nutrients, and I'm very pleased with the barcode scanner to even make easier track of foods. Bottom line, I would recommend to a friend, and already have. My thanks to the developers who created this handy piece of hardware :)

I love this app. Since January, I have been using this tool to get my life back together and lose the 60 pounds I have gained since the covid days. I have also used it in the past when a lot of the features I like were free. I am signed up for their premium subscription with the justification that medical bills for being obese are much more expensive. Anyway, I have lost 40 pounds with the help of this app and have no intent on stopping. I hope they get a food scale that pairs to the app.

I love this app, I use the free version and it provides me everything I am looking for in a dietary tracker. I love that my fitbit and smart scale (ya that's a thing) both sync up seamlessly to keep track of my steps (calories out) and my weight loss progress. I especially appreciate that so many foods /items are on here to search and record. My husband uses another app and constantly asks me what myfitness says... why he doesn't just download this is beyond me... Any way, this is a great app. 👍

It is easy to use, lets you scan in bar codes on many foods, and has a large database of comparable and similar foods to draw from when you need to log something you ate, but don't quite know what to enter. When you need to choose a food similar to what you consumed, it has a lot of options. For routine foods you eat, those pop up quickly, and you just select that item again, which saves you time. If you pay for the subscription, you can export your entire food log to a spreadsheet.

Updating my review. Admittedly, I haven't used this app in a while. I came back to it and find there is no longer an option to scan barcodes unless I pay. I'm not opposed to paying for apps however this function is pretty basic. I realize I can still search for the food I want but often too many other things show before I find what I need. I'll be removing this app. I have Fitbit so I can just use that.

OMG!! This app is PHENOMENAL!😃 It was just a tad bit confusing to navigate through, so I would suggest a short tutorial for folks like me who aren't as gadget savvy 😂. I LOVE how I can connect my other app to track my walking. It just plugs the info right in within seconds! My doctor recommended that I journal everything I eat so and voila, this app does it for you 😃💪. Just about every food is listed with calories, fats, everything! It sux only have room for 500 words. I have more to say 😫

This is a great app if you want to count your calories everyday. Very easy, just like most things the entering of what you do in the beginning is the hardest, but you can scan in the bar code if you eat packaged items. The data base that they pull from must be hug. I enjoy the free mode only and have not tried the paid side.

I came here from Noom and then Weight Watchers. I wasn't here before any changes, so I don't know the difference from the previous ways. I love it here! It super user friendly and the ads aren't even that annoying. It's a free app people, there's gonna be ads. They don't mess me up at all! No weird pop-ups or notifications. I'm down for it. Free-fifty-free baby.

I really aporeciate the access to many different food brands and makers. It's also extremely helpful that I can construct a recipe of my own and however many servings I say it makes and then reference this recipe efficiently and with ease. This app has made losing weight according to calorie counting very feasible, and with some discipline and this well-designed app, I've transitioned into the best physical shape of my life so far. Thank you for helping me, makers of this app.

It is the most complete calorie & fitness tracker I've seen. I'm not a gym rat or a calorie counter. I simply like to track my progress. It has all the food, some by bar scanning the item, and all the exercises as well as being able to link with wearable fitness trackers. Your diary & fitness is shareable with people by code only.

I've been using this app for 4 weeks now and recording my daily food intake(which automatically records calories,carbs,protein,fat,and many more macro-nutrients),as well as calculating effects of excersize. I've lost 40lbs in 3 months and the past month with this app has been great! It keeps me focused on my goal and keeps me accountable to myself.

Full disclosure, this app has a significant glitch with respect to Android OS, whereby it does not sync with their web portal/cloud. This means that if you lost the data on your phone, or purchased a new phone, you would be SOL as none of your data would be able to sync because it does not exist anywhere else other than your phone! For me this is a big deal, but the developer is aware of it and is taking steps to remedy it. Other than this, I give this app a 4/5 (free version:)

Great way to actually log and understand what you are eating. I am a proponent of eating meals that are balanced among the macros. This app tells you breakdown of each food type, meal, day, week or month the amt of protein, fat and carbs im eating. I used this app years ago and it used to allow scanning barcodes on the free version. That is my only complaint.

The new updated UI is a huge, huge step back from the previous one. It now takes so many additional taps to do/see basic features that were immediately visible in the original view. You can no longer see the exact amount of calories burned that it's tracking in an intuitive way. You can't see your full meals either without clicking through the category. A real downgrade 3/5/26 Update - They reverted back to the original look, score updated to reflect that

App works well. some minor improvements are still needed. when I get a reminder to log a meal, it should take me to that meal page. It always seems to miss that. I'll get a reminder to log breakfast, then it takes me to the lunch or dinner page. also, they could do some work to de-dupe redundant food options.

Not a huge fan of the recent update/change. Feel like I need to relearn the app. 1 or 2 issues I really don't like. The intermittent fasting button is no longer on the dashboard for easy start/stopping. I'm trying to figure out if it can be added but can't find how. When updating times, wish I could check which items to update or a select all option instead of going one by one.

I need to file a bug report for an issue. I'm on the most current version of Android on a Pixel 7 Pro. Whenever it click on a notification to log a meal, itakes me into the app for the next meal. So if it's a reminder to log breakfast, I end up with lunch selected when I click through the notification. Lunch clicks into dinner. Dinner into snacks. It's been this way for almost as long as I've been using the app, which is going on a year and a half now. So lots of versions of the app, OS, patch

I have honestly really loved this app and have been using it for quite a while! My only big issue is that whenever I log exercise it increases my calorie limit for the day and I don't like that. I would like to keep my limit as is. Also, now I magically can't see macros for my meal without buying premium. I have been using the free version for 105 days. I have always been able to see my macros. This is an unnecessary and a bit greedy change to make that makes me feel very disappointed with it.

For 14+ years I've used the free version because I don't like to change. After being sold at least twice it's benefits have continued to decline. I can't help but think if they hadn't taken away the basic features every other nutrition app offers for free, more people would try it out and eventually purchase the plus version. Oh well, bad management I guess.

pretty cool app to count estimated calories and other info. But the syncing time between health connect, Fitbit, and this app is terrible. I've done so much research on figuring out how to get everything synced more than once a day. but it takes almost all day for this app to sync the steps, therefore it's a bit tricky to figure out how many calories I can consume when it's not calculating the calories I burned from my steps.

Restoring the former scoring as the latest problem with the app wasn't really from the app itself but from AWS latest outage. The app is quite convenient and helps with the food tracking and macros calculation. The only thing that I would add in here is have feature parity between the web and mobile UI when it comes to search among my own recipes. While the mobile option let you do a search with keywords, the web one doesn't and that is quite frustrating.

Lots of great features in this app! I would love to see more functionality in the friends feature. I want to see if my friends are hitting their goals, and for them to see mine, so that we can encourage each other. It would also be nice to see what they are eating especially when we eat together. It can be a hassle to put each ingredient myself. If my buddy literally ate the same thing, why duplicate all that work?

I started using the free version of the app, but decided to try the 30 day trial of the Premium version. I find the app to be very helpful tracking food, water intake and weight. The activity tracking has a few glitches for me though since my Amazon Amazfit smart watch isn't compatible with the app. I use Google Fit and sync it to the app with Google Health Connect. I find it to be frustrating as it doesn't connect in real time. I would give 5 stars otherwise.

Great app that helped me tremendously to reach my goal weight and stay at a healthy weight. but seeing up the syncing is complicated, and today, it stopped properly syncing. Weight hasn't synced for a while, and I tried to fix that, but now it only shows incorrect calorie counts from Health Connect, EVEN THOUGH THAT APP IS NOT CONNECTED. It does not show the Fitbit count like it used to.

Health Connect keeps duplicating or triplicating my workouts. I have Garmin connected to MFP and they keep bouncing workouts back and forth until it looks like I've burned thousands of calories that I haven't. I can't turn it off without disconnecting Garmin which I prefer to track my workouts. Fix it, please! Google Pixel 6

I really like the ease of the app and the tracking abilities of progress, exercise, and calorie intake. I'm not giving it 5 stars because a lot of the paid version features show up and when you click on them it asks you to upgrade. I understand why but I think it should just show you the features you have for the version you have.

Been using the free version of this since September 2025 and here's the pros and cons. Pro: - Allows you to easily track calorie count by either typing the food name or just inserting the calorie count if able. - Includes a stack of recipes that actually taste good. Cons: - Tracking for Fat, carbs and protein is locked behind a paywall - Can't bookmark recipes without a premium subscription. - Way too many features are locked behind a paywall.

i love the app, helps me stay on track and easy to use. they did recently have an update with new looks and i feel like its not as user friendly. the 'swipe' to each day was so easy to skim through as opposed to now having to click the day, go back and click a different week etc. the foods also arent listed in each category on the front page, you have to click the category to see the rest of the foods . 🫤

I gave this a 5 star rating because it has helped me so tremendously. There's only 1 thing that could be better. If I could sync my accupedo pedometer and also log other exercise. Right now it is either or situation. Now that I have used it for several months, I really have a feel for my overall nutrition intake. But it has become more tedious than it should to enter foods because there are so many in my recent list. Several duplicates. The search feature could w

Most of the app works well for my needs. If I can scan the barcode of the food I am eating, that is the best scenario. My only complaint is that it offers too many suggestions of varying calories for the exact same item I enter as a search. For example, if I enter "raspberries" in the search bar, the result is at least 10 different variations of calories for raspberries. For foods that I enter often or scan often, I wish these would pop up as a preferred/often food/calories when doing a search.

The database of foods is astonishing, and the functionality makes tracking food, weight, water, and exercise easy. With it, I was able to lose about 35 lbs in about six months, simply through rigorous tracking. I take a star off for two reasons. The first is for some bugs, including not being able to get input from my pedometer app (which is claimed to be supported and worked initially, but ceased and cannot be reestablished). The second reason is the incessant marketing. Near daily advertising for UA products gets a bit annoying. I might be more receptive if it wasn't so frequent. But it's basic human biology that the body and mind eventually drown out constant stimuli.

Very helpful in many ways, it keeps me accountable for all the food I put in my body. I'm successfully losing weight at a healthy pace! My only frustration (besides making us pay for the things that used to be free with the app-looking at YOU barcode scanner OR the excessive ads to upgrade), it doesn't always sync smoothly with my smart watch app (Garmin connect). It won't sync across both platforms. Kind of annoying, but on the whole I recommend this app to others.

I've used MFP off and on for 6 years now and have had great success tracking. However, I'm bummed to see the barcode scanner is now a premium feature (previously free). This allowed me to track quicker and more accurately, without manually adding or relying on the (not always accurate) database. Would love to see this become a free feature again!

I used this app many years ago and now after getting a Garmin I'm using it again - it's come a long way! Much more intuitive... I do wish that there were alarms that you could set for the beginning and end of a fast, and I wish that you could start a fast and back-time it and then just keep it running, and instead of having to submit that you've ended your fast, it would be nice if you could just record your first food and it would automatically end the fast...

This is an impressive app in that you can keep it simple or go all out. So far I'm just using this to track calories. I haven't used than that, but that has been working well for me, and I've had little trouble finding or adding the foods I eat - their database is huge! The updates so far have fixed my biggest complaints, so it gets 4 stars to start with. The devs are obviously on the ball! If I get around to checking out the other features, I'll update my review and likely add that final star.

I've been using this for maybe 3-4 years now. I've lost 65 pounds and maintained. But I just wish they didn't make the original features "paid". For instance the ability to scan a label and add the item. That's now only paid and have to search and hope you find the item or similar and sometimes the manual entries are definitely suspect and seem inaccurate. So that's unfortunate. Otherwise pretty good all around.

(Free version) So far, so good with some hiccups. Have used this app with no problems for about a month. Very easy to use. Can find almost every food I look up. But...this last update, started having problems. Won't sync with my Watch to log my steps. As well as having difficulty adding new recipes. Even with that, still love the app to give me the heads up on what I'm eating every day to help with my weightloss journey.

As of 1/7/19: A recent update seems to have caused some network errors with the app. I'm unable to search for meals, food, etc. as it just fails and gives me a network error. The new update I just installed hasn't done anything yet. This app is very easy to use and understand. It helps me keep an eye on how much I eat, what I'm eating, and how I can improve. Not only that; it can log any of my activities that I take part in, too.

I've absolutely loved the app, and it has helped me with healthier eating habits. That being said, as of April 2024, the app does not seem to be working for me anymore. Despite uninstalling and reinstalling the app several times. When launching the app, it stays indefinitely on the blue opening screen and cannot get it to work, so I've had to use the desktop app for my tracking which is a little more inconvenient when on the go...

I used this app a year ago and the ads weren't nearly as intrusive as they are now that is the only reason I don't give 5 stars. I don't like having to click ignore the premium membership every time I open a new page. The ability to save meals and recipes as well as a huge library of foods to choose from for tracking is great though.

This has almost everything! The two things I'd love to have is: 1. Capability to see my net carbs for each individual meal and total day nutrition view. 2. I'd like to have the option to see something other than calories. Example, I'd like to see macros plus net carbs vs. macros and calories. Other than that, wow! I love being able to scan my foods with UPC codes! The scan option has found all but one food which I was able to add. Any restaurants that don't have the menu I'm able to find online

Overall the app is good. Very large library of foods/nutritional info. Good interface, easy to use. Two main cons: 1) I don't mind having ads, but the way they do them is a little glitchy. It takes a half second to initiate the ad, so you're in the middle of typing when they pop up, and 2) The ads have close buttons that are hidden or hard to find. They shouldn't be trying to trick us in the app like this.

It's nice being able to keep up with what you are eating, just look very closely at what you are adding. The vast majority of the foods that are on the app have the wrong nutritional info. Don't just scan a barcode and move on, look to see if it is correct. I'mhappily surprised when I scan one and it is correct. I've pretty much given up reporting foods because they never update it. Edit: I want to add that I'm not going to a website to update a food when the app has a report function.

This app has helped me keep track of what I eat. So far, I have lost 22 pounds since Jan 3. However, it is a bit frustrating when the numbers come up wrong when scanning a barcode. But, thankfully, I can edit the item and save it to my list. Thanks MyFitnessPal for being a good assistant!! 70 more pounds to go!!

The "net calories" notion is erroneous from the point of TDEE tracking. Calorie inputs should not be modified based on calories burned. This is misleading and should be ignored and factored into the initial calorie calculation. This is why I am taking a star away. Rating the app- it is very stable and has a robust database. Besides the cluttering with articles one rarely reads, it's straightforward and good to use on a daily basis to track your macros. Would recommend.

Updated 7/27/24 - after exploring other options, MFP seems to have the most accurate data base, and does not require a subscription to synchronize with other apps like Garmin, Fitbit, etc. For these reasons I will continue using MFP. 7/24/24 - I hate that there is no newsfeed anymore. My friends and I cannot "like" our workouts or celebrate log in anniversaries. 6/12/14 - Randomly freezes. Occasionally have to uninstall then reinstall. Good diet app in general.

Update 4/2/22: Why did you take away the multi-add function with the last update? I hate not being able to see what I have already logged and it slows me down when trying to use the app. Very frustrating. Lots of food/calorie information in it's database makes it easy to track calories and nutrition. I've used it on and off over the years and I'm much better at managing my weight when I use it.

It is extremely useful for calorie count and portion control, as well as tracking your weight. Most of the interesting features are not free, such as further monitoring of macros, or adding exercise. I love they suggest reading the blog, there is something for everyone, at least an interesting beginning. I love adding foods using the scanner, or being able to add a favorite food and keeping a list of foods consumed by me. This app was recommended to me by a personal trainer and I recommended this app to my mother in law for calorie count. I would like to see a video /s of what this app can do, for different people: kids going to the gym, mom trying to lose weight, and grandma try not gaining more details

I really like this app for taking food, exercise, and weight. The bar-code scanner in the food log makes looking up many products easy, and the community has helped both fill in the database, and accuracy check it. My one complaint is how much the ads eat my battery and data allotment. I'd gladly pay a fee to be rid of them. Heck, I'd pay a fee to just be able to restrict them to download only via Wi-Fi. Edit 30-Jan-2020: Corrected typos and spelling error. Original evaluation was some time prior to 2015.

This is my first fitness app. It is awesome for tracking my carbs . App is somewhat user friendly. I still wander around to find what I want. An improvement would be to show what foods contain sugar in my daily diet. It is challenging to identify the actual food I ate. often by default, I input the incorrect food and my stats are then slightly incorrect. Then I hunt for what is incorrect. I continue to learn how to use this app and look forward to downloading reports. I would recommend this app.

It's a good way to track your food and weigt. The premium version can be better to start as it's easier to scan foods, but after they are in your account, they stay, so then it's easy to switch to the free version. The exercise routines are a useful guide for tracking calories burned, but I find they over estimate how many calories you've burned (sometimes by a lot) so you might want to check with other sources before logging an exercise. It's easy to edit calories burned though.

Good app. It used to have a multi select option to more easily put items from one source together. For example, you could search "Starbucks" and the entire Starbucks menu would pop up. Then you could click the items you ate/drank. This sounds like a nit-pick, but I went to Pieology and had to re enter "Pieology" for every topping I put on the pizza. Also, double check the nutrition information on your entries. I have found lots of mistakes.

Great App! Easy way to meet your health goals by scanning on the go! it has enough use to have a large database of foods already added, and very easy to add if it's not there. It joins with your fitness tracker to give credit for steps. Keep up the great work team! Going from good to great, it would be nice to translate workouts in your preferred fitness tracker to here, and not double credit.

Pretty good app. Not typically a fan of regular subscription for premium services (unless it's somehow essential, it usually means it's a money grab for greedy companies, especially since much of the food data appears crowdsourced) as opposed to a pay once model and if I see one like that I'll switch to that, but for what this does it does it well.

I think there are some limitations. I have found a few foods that are not accurate in their names and portion sizes. I would like to be able to track net carbs without the extra clicks. I want to be able to enter my macros and account for my IF. There's more I could add as feedback. These are off the top of my head. Overall, I like the app minus the paid version.

There are some cool features, but for the expense, I would hope for a cleaner user experience. There are so many verified ingredients that have wrong nutritional information listed it can be a challenge to sift through in order to build a correct calorie count. I like the easy and simplicity of the app and the quick add and barcode scanner make it easier.

Worked just fine until today when, like a lot of other reviewers have reported, it kicked me out and I can't log back in. It makes me go through the whole registration process and then at the end says there's an error establishing the username or words to that effect. I hope my info can be restored...it'll be a pain to have to start over with another app, plus it was encouraging to see the weightloss progress I've made since August. I guess I can at least be glad the pounds didn't pile back on when the app broke... Edit: app is.workimg again so I adjusted stars accordingly.

Updated to 3 stars. Downloaded again after years, now anything I want to do it just prompts me to pay for the premier. Sad to see a good app go the way of sheer greed. Uninstalling. Love that it syncs to my Bowflex. Calorie count is easy and fast; database is extensive, although I'm not sure how reliable some are. Wish it would sync directly to my phone's pedometer/excercise tracker rather than needing a separate app like Pacer.

I haved used this app for many years as a daily diary. It does a what I need to track my calories and runs. My dissatisfaction is their monthly cost for premium. If it were $6-7 monthly like most subscriptions, I would support the application. But instead they want a subscription fee on par with Office 365 or Adobe cloud, so I'm constantly looking for another application.

I am a 43 year old Male who on July 22nd, 2019 underwent Bypass Surgery, five to be exact, so clearly I was not taking care of myself. Since then I have making a lot of, hopefully long-term, positive life/health changes. I was previously, and to some degree still am, using the Samsung Health app. By far MyFitnessPal is far superior with tracking eating habits. Only thing I wish was that it was "easier" to track walking, exercise, etc. Wish I could expand on both topics more but I'm out of..

App is great. It has a great database of foods, drinks to choose. It helps me with my daily intake. 2 things. It does send reminders that I didn't enter whatever meal I had, even though I did. Minor annoyance. But I do wish they had a vitamin section. I just put my daily regimen in the snacks. And I wish that we could group select items to add, instead of browsing through the list. It has a history, so if you consume something, you can add it easily. Minor nitpicking.

Fitness Pal has been a great help by keeping track of my calories and my macro information. The pie graph is great. The only thing I have trouble with is when I eat a homemade recipe. I cannot individually enter the calories, carbs, fat & protein unless the program can find the name in its database. If it's possible to import recipes, without going to Premium, I haven't figured out how. Almost meets expectations. Don't know what lockscreen is.

I have only one irritation with this app. I have ADHD, and on my own, I will forget to log my meals. Every. Single. Day. So, I went into the app and asked it to remind me to log them, setting it for two reminders a day, around lunch and suppertime. It doesn't remind me at those times. In fact, it almost never reminds me at all, and when it does, it's at random, like 2 a.m. or some other time when I really don't need it. Other than that, it's perfect for my needs, and is easy to use.

Love the food database and recipe connection. I've tried 100 times to connect to Misfit with tech help even. It doesn't work. It doesn't even connect to it's own partner apps nine times out of ten. If it did this would be a great app. - Update I got a Garmin instead of a Misfit and now there are fewer steps to connect but still not a great experience. A way to sort the recipes would add a lot.

I've been using this App since 2013. I recommitted in 2018 to get my weight & lifestyle habits on track. In 2019 I decided I challenged myself to log daily. I'm now over 500 days. The App is very easy to use & manage. Lately, though, I am disappointed by the fact that I'm having to take extra steps to log. Instead of being able to log the meal, I need to go to the left sidebar and click food diary. If not, the screen I receive is one about nutrients etc. Can't afford premium.

Very excellent app once you get used to navigate through it. It does have adds, but they are relevant to fitness. I love the calories counter, and the app will notify you if you forget to log. However having difficult time with syncing with the other fitness apps. Other than that, it's been very helpful.

I enjoy using this app. Would love to be able not to have to re-enter same foods that I eat every day. I have one complaint, the app does not use net carbs, so I have to recalculate by hand. I've considered finding another app due to this even though I'm happy with the function of this one. Another great addition would be the ability to track blood glucose in the app too. I actually considered purchasing the full app until I saw what they wanted monthly, nope.

i am new to counting calories but this app makes it easy. Be honest or it wont help. I am learning more about food, nutrition, and exercise with this app. I honestly feel a new life style is happening. I always worked out 5 days a week but now i am taking it to the next level. No calorie credit for strength training but I think that is just the way it is.

Overall, this is a great tool to track food, etc. My primary complaint and concern is that there is so many, and mostly inaccurate, entries to choose from. I understand that this is a free app for the most part but it is almost useless unless you research entries and create your own. It is a lot of work to get your common foods in the system for use.

Overall, the app has helped educate me on the food I eat. In the past, I cut portions to lose weight but that only got me so far. The app showed me it is also worth considering what I eat being the issue. My main problem with the app is the step count. Sometimes, I struggle with getting a proper step count to sync. I have used Pacer and Fitbit over the last year and found that updates to one of these apps or devices often would lead to issues until Under Armour makes updates to this app

Not a lot of restaurants in here and I rarely seem to be able to find the exact food I am eating so I have to guess or something. Certain foods are easy to find, the scanner is super helpful. However, I don't think it's all that accurate since there is so much variation between brands and the size of food but it's good for estimating calories at least.

Hardest part is being disciplined and logging in the info. but it does help track food intake. I do like that I can pull up random foods and get estimates. Never would think curry rotti would be in the app, but it was. I know it's an estimate but it let's u guage your calories. I am using the free feature. One thing that tho, sometimes the galaxy watch logs on twice. Took me awhile and figured out how to not have that happen (but not sure what I did, but haven't had an issue since).

Editing my review, previously 3 stars, due to the fact that they took barcode scanning out of the free version. I've been using MFP for quite some time now, and I have also done Weight Watchers. I must say that I like MFP much better. I just decided to sign up for premium, because with a discount code I was able to get it for $59.99 a year. The list price is too high, so I hope they don't ever actually charge that much. Overall, great app. Look for a discount code if you want premium.

I know tracking food intake is essential for weight management. This app has provided valuable insight and guidance as well as a fairy easy logging interface. I have not yet upgraded to premium and cannot comment on any additional value. The comment check boxes ask about disruptive ads. I honestly answered no. There are many ads. Some totally useless. I have no problem accepting them as the price of using this app.

I have been using this app for 4 years. Overall, it's reliable, but like this week, it has been so Clichy adding food I didn't add. It also is saying I need to lower my fat macros even though I didn't change the grams I'm consuming. It's very frustrating when you are trying to be as accurate as possible for a cut. It makes it very confusing.

Great app, very convenient and easy to use. I've never been able to count calories before as it's such a pain, but the barcode scanner has made it easy! Only issue is it sometimes duplicates foods, or won't input for some drinks even though they're in the database they won't show up in my meals when I press the check mark.

This app is very helpful for tracking your food intake, water, nutrients, as well as exercise among other things. There is an extensive range of exercises and a large food database that enables you to log practically anything you've eaten or done. I would definitely recommend this app to anyone looking to eat healthier, lose weight, or even maintain their weight.

I like MFP. I started using it about 6 years ago to help me stay on track to lose weight over the course of a year for a wedding I was going to be in. Had I stayed with it, I would probably be much healthier! Even though I haven'tbused the app in years, it still has all my login info and welcomed me back. This is a good "self serve" type of app. If you need more guidance like what to eat and when to eat it, you may want to try something else. If you have a pretty good idea of what you are doing and just need an accountability measure, then this app is great! If you want to pay, I think things get more specialized, but I may never know because I plan to continue only with the free version.

This app is beneficial when it comes to counting your calories and how much one should be consuming on a daily basis. It has the food options that can estimate the calorie intake of your food choices that can help you keep track. The only thing that needs some more work is having more restaurants added to the list so that everything can be tracked with the accurate amount of carbs, calories, and nutrients.

Pros: *HUGE data bank of foods *easy to enter recipes *very user friendly and intuitive Cons: *Have never been able to get Fitbit to sync with MFP and show up in the place it's supposed to be *a lot of the foods are in grams, so without a scale you're out of luck * I can't figure out how to use the message boards. That's prob a *me* problem, not a MFP problem.

It has been a good app to use, even the free version. I upgraded to premium and that made logging food easier. That is about all I use it for. I would like for someone or a team at my fitness pal to remove the MANY duplicated and inaccurate foods. But, the more you log, the easier it is, it will remember your recent and regular items logged.

Because this app is completely free, I give it 4 stars. I use it every day, it syncs with my Fitbit, overall it works well. The reason it doesn't get 5 stars is because a lot of the food entries have incorrect calorie and nutrition information, so sometimes it can be frustrating trying to find the right one, you have to pay attention as you enter food into your diary to make sure it's accurate. The app itself is user friendly, easy enough to use, but has a couple glitchy things here or there.

it's great! it helps a lot. The only thing us that you kinda have to log in to everything and search for everything to get more accurate results. This is especially the case with exercise, since you have to add in all the factors involved in your routine, and if you watched a workout video, you'd have so search for the names of the positions of everything.

Have not been able to sign in recently. I out my login info in and it says please wait then nothing happens. Been having to input food on my laptop not great especially after paying for premium. Edit: was able to get in touch with customer support and they have fixed the problem. I now have no issues with logging in. The app is a great accountability tool.

This is an update! My main goal with this app is to count calories/document a food diary. That part of the app works great. I would like to move items between days to make it easier to document meals like breakfast which are the same many times. have been away for a few years. There is more content but you have to sign up for a premium account. I think there should be more free content here and less attempts to have me sigb up for a premium account.

I've used this app for years. i love it but wish you didn't have to go premium for certain features. I also like that if i miss a log i can reset it. I wish that the app would allow for edits when you make a status and realize you misspelled something. I miss the scan barcode feature to log food on the free app. But all in all my fitness pal keeps me accountable

The app has worked really well, I've been using it for about half a year. It has been very smooth to log foods and exercises. Just there is one problem and I don't know if It's just me. Recently I have noticed that my steps have not been logging correctly. It would only log that I have burnt 1 calorie. Also before this connecting steps has always been glitchy. Otherwise the app is great and a good option if you want to log your calories.

I have done many food diaries, but using an app on my phone is by far the easiest. My doctor recommended this app, so I tried it and really like it. I do wish the full version was not by subscription but instead a one-time cost. Subscriptions are unwieldy to deal with when you want to cancel or have to change payment options.

This app is great! Collection of key information on staying on track with your food, exercise, weight, etc... Especially that it can link to other apps that track specific data such as Samsung Health! I wish it would link to the Body Fast app, or offered a fasting tracking like it as it would make this app even better! I don't need to be reminded of meals I'm skipping due to fasting.

I've love using MyFitnessPal from the very start. I've enjoyed using features such as intermittent fasting tracker to building my own recipes for future meal tracking. It's been a game changer for my personal health journey. I had previously rated as a 5 star. However, the app now requires me to log back in constantly to add diary entries whereas in the past, this was not the case. I've tried multiple recommendations to which none have resolved the situation.

The counter is great for staying on track with calories, water, and exercise goals. It is easy to navigate and use. My only issue is that my FitBit and MFP's apps will not synch, so it has been impossible to adequately track my exercise and steps each day. This is annoying and frustrating because I average 15k-20k steps a day and have no way of seeing what that does for my calorie count.

Lets you set a calorie goal and count them by food/meal/day and has a fairly intuitive interface. Defaults are strange: daily RDA isn't weighted by calorie goal (always 100%) and has a goal for bad fats but not good fats. Still needs dark mode and has bugs like telling you a custom food's nutrition info is wrong without saying what and then accepting that info anyway on the second try. Also doesn't distinguish between foods and supplements, which require a calorie count and are called "food".

search features are quite impressive and the barcode scanner is super useful. The only downside in my opinion is the creating meals can be a hassle if you plan to eat different portions of the same stuff. you would essentially have to create an entire new meal to because of the different grams or ounces of food. I have found that creating simple meals with no more than 2 items because editing and having to search again for items can be time consuming.

I love this app, def one of the best calorie logging apps I've used. however, they locked some of their best features up behind the premium recently, which i wouldn't even mind, if wasn't $20/ month?!? So bar code scanning is now premium (this feature allows you to scan the barcode on food packaging and it automatically bring up the nutrition facts.) Macros have been an upgraded feature for a while, so I have no idea if there app is any good at macros since I've never had premium

This app has a wide range of foods in its database so I do not need to manually type in any of the nutritional facts. Also I like that there are challenges and macronutrient breakdowns available. It's also easy to track my goals and progress. There are additional features (like plans and more in-depth nutrient breakdowns) that are only available after purchase. Still, I am pleased with the things I'm able to do with the free version of this app.

Great App, but..... I used this app a few years ago and it was great! Scan barcodes for your foods, build your own meals to log them easily.
