I just re-download and started using again, and adding the foods is very glitchy! After entering a food and clicking the check mark, it doesn't add it to your day, or it shows later as 3 or 4 separate entries! Also, foods from previous day are added to the next day already. Please fix.

This app helps me keep on track and lets me interact with my friends which makes losing weight more fun and effective!! The only thing I dislike is when I add my exercise and calories burned it wants me to eat more food at the end of the diary. I normally can't eat more food, so there are times it will not let me complete my diary. Other than that, this App is very helpful in my daily routine and health goals!

Good app for tracking what you are eating and what exercise you get. Many foods are in the database and make it easy to add what you've eaten without putting in all the nutritional info yourself. Premium isn't constantly shoved in your face which is nice and ads aren't very obtrusive, though a few of the things locked behind paying are odd [like checking foods you've eaten that are the highest in a certain thing]

I used this app about 7 years ago (it was free back then) when I worked out seriously and lost a lot of weight. I loved it when I used it. I have some health issues now and want to work out again and track my food. I was disappointed to find out just how expensive this app is now. It is a great app but not quite that much. It would be great if it could be updated to include optional glucose tracking as well. Many people with pre diabetes or diabetes also work out and monitor their food.

Pros: Love the barcode scanner. Makes it so quick to enter foods. Like the nutrition profile too. Good amount of remonders to log food and info about the food as you go. Cons: Don't understand why weight training doesn't show as any calories burned at all. Also, my Fitbit is connected to it but nothing I do there shows up here. Not sure if that's a glitch on this side or Fitbit's side. Would be nice if there was a way to enter notes to self as well. Overall, great app.

Tracks food & water for accountability (free version); rate higher if, when I delete item after adding a saved meal to the next day, it didn't add it right back-have to delete it twice (weird quirk); & doesn't always have (dry) ounce measurements on some foods; & 'multi-add' should be default. Good blog articles, can check my nutrition, see weight trends. Finds previous items by typing a few letters to bring up suggestions. Can add new foods to database. TY!

It's a useful tool for weight loss. Very easy to use, the barcode reader is a plus in the premium program. The one thing that could use improvement is the recipe creator. It would be easier if you could scan your bulk items making it faster and more accurate. Regardless I have done other programs such as Weight Watchers, but this is cheaper and doesn't require leaving home for meetings and weigh-ins for success. Just being personally responsible.

It's a good app to count calories and chat with friends. Adding exercise is very difficult for example I went to a strength class tonight. No way to add 1 hour of strength training. Would literally have to put each exercise we did and the number of reps. Bottom line they want you to buy premium and will make you miserable with ads and locked out features until you do.

The app is useful and you can scan barcodes which is helpful and usually accurate. I only use it to keep track of calories right now and not proteins/fats/fiber so I can't say much about those features. My biggest annoyance with it is the "Add frequently paired foods" list when you view a food item. I have cream, milk, and sugar every morning in my coffee and somehow they often don't show up in this list. I don't know how it's written but it's definitely not as helpful as it could be.

App has helped me keep track of how much i eat every day and if i exercise i get a few more calories that i can eat maybe a little something else. 😊 it has helped me lose weight in the past. It holds me accountable for what i eat. The downside logging all ingredients for a dish. Every so often it wont sync with my fitbit. Sometimes the app with just shut off while im logging my food.

I use the app primarily for tracking my meals and keeping track of calories. For that, it's pretty good. I use a fitness tracker that's not partnered with them though, so I can't actually log my steps manually without having to go some roundabout way. I think the app overall would be better if you could use whatever tracker you have instead of one of the expensive fitbits or garmin trackers.

It's a good motivator for consumption control AND exercise if you are diligent about entering data. Some of the exercise info is confusing but I've only had it for 2 weeks. Should add things like snow shoveling, raking (larger physical activities not perfirmed daily. GREAT food list with brands available. Overall nice app... I'm an IT idiot and I'm figuring it out!

Good app. Easy to track food. It would be easier to track if more foods had volume measurements (cups, teaspoons, tablespoons) in addition to grams and ounces. I find it easier to identify volume measurements rather than weight across a large variety of foods. As is, no complaints and I find it easy to track across meals. It also remembers frequent ingredients to make tracking faster.

The app is easy to use. I used to hate tracking but I have to admit, this app has been so helpful during my weightloss journey. My fitness coach recommended it. I'm so glad I started using. She's able to keep me accountable because once I connected with her she can see what I track (food, exercise, weight). The app gives me a better understanding of what I'm eating. This has been an awesome tool that has truly helped me to lose weight successfully!

Overall very good app, love the fact that I can connect the exercise tracker to my plan. The reminder to log my meals comes in handy. Not happy that I have to pay for some items, but I understand the developer should make some money. Looking up food items is tricky at times and I usually find something close. I'm only a week in and I find it very helpful and opens my eyes to my poor habits. Good luck to everyone on their fitness journey!

I have been using MyFitnessPal for over a year, without missing a day! It has helped me immensely to get a handle on my eating habits, make better choices, and balance my macros. I have lost 48 pounds! I have learned some new recipes, and created others in the app. That being said, there are glitches here and there in functionality. For example, I must tap on "diary" icon to access the diary, rather than tapping on the diary itself. Overall, though, it is a great app.

App is running worse & worse, day by day. This app used to be the best. I recently installed MFP because I started a serious weight loss goal. At first it was working perfectly. I'm familiar with MFP because I had used it 5 years ago to lose weight & it 100% helped me reach my goal then. Now, the app is VERY unreliable. It never picks up my steps from my fitness watch or other apps like Map My Fitness. It's adding exercises on days I've done zero (couch potato days). Very, very unreliable.

Good but features keep becoming premium. This morning I went to enter my food, and after I searched and found what I wanted, entered the quantity and went to add it to my diary it wouldn't respond. I've tried closing and reopening the app. uninstalling and reinstalling it. The only thing I can figure is that now the food diary has become a premium feature as well. Very quickly becoming a useless service if you don't want to pay top money for what other apps give for free.

UPDATE: I had to take this down to 3 stars now because as of how this app has become BOMBARDED with ads AND you can no longer scan barcodes without paying for the premium version of the app which also, by the way, removes the ads. How convenient! Otherwise it's a good app for tracking your food entries and viewing your nutritional intake. Those ads are very annoying though. If the cost wasn't so high I would consider purchasing just to remove them.

the new user interface was not designed with the user in mind. We want to see a comparison of our macros, our meals laid out, and our water intake at least. The new UI is oversimplified so you can't really get any data out of the main view without clicking into each category. The designers should remember the "why" of the app when doing the design.

I feel its over all easy to use. for being the most recommended food log app im surprised at how inconsistent it is. the food calories are all over the bored. I have to good first. and then find the one closest. the my recipe tab is not what I like. you cant edit recipes once finished. I've messed up on a few and I want to edit them. over all WW was an amazing app and its still tracking calories/macros, just with a points scale. I would pay for my fitness pal if it was a smoother app like WW.

March 2026, this app, in the free version, keeps removing features and saying to do "this", go premium. If it weren't my history logging and progress since 2015, I would delete this app and move on. You can't log any meals except the present day. If you miss a day, you can't go back and you lose your streak. I went on a cruise, had no internet package. so I kept track of steps, meals, snacks, and alcohol to log when I got home. Nope, lost that feature while overseas!

Been using for almost a year. It won't let me save the recipe I imported. I tried to manually enter it, it won't save that either. I cleared my phone cache, I uninstalled the app, and reinstalled. Still the same thing. It won't be of any use to me, if I can't enter my own recipe or import one. This just started, as I was able to enter a recipe earlier in the day.

I've used this app for years and have been happy with it (free version). Now for some reason, the app has been logging me out and disconnecting my apps for exercise tracking. I have to log back in and reconnect the apps about every other day. UPDATE: I've uninstalled and reinstalled the app and still having the same issue. Having to relog in every other day and it drops my connected exercise app (Google Fit). I suppose it's time to find a new app that works.

The light gray subtext on the dark theme is very difficult to read and doesn't stand out. Please consider changing the colors to something easier to read. Also, listing out logged foods as bullet points is better than a single line with half of it hidden so I can quickly see what I logged or missed for each meal.

I would say the base functions are great. do not pay for the premium version. barcode scanning works well, but voice logging is a pile of dog doo doo. I'll say grams it'll put ounces, it'll put the wrong thing, I end up spending more time overall because I'm trying twice with the voice log and then I end up logging it the free way anyway. Just deal with the ads honestly

Ambiguous navigation, incomplete "verified" nutrition information, yet they are really pushing the premium service. It's true there are many foods in the database, but you have to be careful what you select for real accuracy! Extremely "goal" oriented, this app will confidently convince you you are doing great based on incorrect data. Perfect for people with low impulse control but can be frustrating if you have nutrients to calculate.

keeps making me login in again every time I exit the app at all. even if I don't fully close it. also can't even find the simple list of foods I ate that day. it only tells me total calories. not where any of them came from what food when nothing. and still annoyed the barcode scanner which had been free since the start of this app is suddenly a paid feature. way to say screw you to long term users.

Calorie counting is excellent. Syncing with Google Fit and Health Connect is extremely inconsistent. It syncs all day just fine until about 4 pm and then it just stops syncing. This problem happens every day. I've taken all of the steps to disconnect and then reconnect etc. I've cleared caches, rebooted the phone etc. but the problem doesn't go away.

The app has a lot of great features that help you lose weight and get healthy. unfortunately I only gave them 3 stars because I believe their billing methods are shady. once you sign up for premium just hope you dont have any issues. they are quick to bill you but forget ever reaching customer service. instead you will get an email to choose an article to read. Basically fix the problem yourself. Gone are the days of decent customer service. I had to go through my Google account to cancel.

Saved Foods are still being deleted. Still having to re-create food entries. Even foods I use every day. Have become used to it though. The other thing is, when you add a food, you enter the Vitamin D for each food. But in the Nutrition tab at the bottom of the daily Diary, it doesn't even show Vitamin D in daily totals. It shows all other categories entered when you create a food except Vitamin D. Other than those two issues, this is a good app. Really like that you can use it offline as well.

The app functions, it has a large catalog of foods and you can add your own, and it has helped me lose some weight. There are no external pop-up ads, but the app is an ad in itself. Every page is littered with begging and pleading to fork over $80 a year. You're shown examples of what you'll get if you pay for premium, and a pop-up giving you a "deal" every time you open the app for $60 a year. This app takes advantage of weight insecurity and I would not touch it if it didn't have Garmin links

Finally popped for the premium version and it wasn't working right from the start. I had it synced to my fitbit but it wouldn't record the calories I burned working out. I would enter my work out manually and when I can back later it would be gone. The feature for taking pictures of food wasn't working right either, there was no button to take the picture with. Very disappointed.

I've been using the free version on and off for years. I It's very handy. The annoying part is the streak notifications - it will congratulate you anytime you log more than two days in a row, and the button to continue or x out of it is so low and high, that they're difficult to click. I do not need a congrats button every single day. I just want to log my food and move on with my day. It is so annoying and there seems to be no way to turn it off. Besides that, the app is decent.

I'm moving this back to a 3 star. I've been a paying subscriber for years and there have been changes but the latest revision, seems like you have to click a button a half a dozen times just to get to the scanner or just to get to the list to log something you've recently logged. Also the recently logged doesn't keep as long a list as it used too. It's slower to maneuver through the pages. Now when I set my macros, they don't stay. Might be time to change.

The good: The barcode scanner is super convenient. I like that I can customize my macros I like that I can log foods in different measurements (cups, grams, oz, etc). The recent update is frustrating. You used to be able to copy a meal from a few days before, but now you can only copy yesterday's meal. And you used to be able to move foods between meals, but now you have to delete each item and then load it on the meal where you want it.

I have used this off and on, I was about to start up again when I learned that you need to have a premium account to use the barcode scanner for food entry. this was among the top features for me, and while I understand the reasoning behind making this a premium ability I am not willing to purchase a subscription to regain it... I'd consider paying a flat fee but I refuse to fall into the financial pit of monthly fees for apps.

Basic and counterintuitive with its layout, but otherwise it's pretty good for what it's worth, I've lost nearly 100 lbs! But the app's been getting more unstable these past few months. It's very laggy, the search bar shuffles around text as I type, I can't search for my own foods/meals anymore, and I keep getting spontaneously get signed out. Yesterday I got signed out while using the app, and it reset some of my settings. It's getting frustrating and I'm considering switching apps.

Awesome app, but unfortunately customer support is lacking. The premium plus features truly are exceptional. Was very frustrating when clicking the meal planner started crashing the app every time as I had come to rely on it for all meal plans, grocery shopping and recipes.I had purchased all the ingredients but no longer had the meal plans or recipes to complete my week as planned and couldn't get my next week started. Wish I could have gotten help instead of a refund. Might repurchase though.

I have been using this since 2013, and several years in a row now, but the new changes are mind boggling. Was there really demand for people to NOT want to be able to see what they ate at each meal, but just have a list of food? Update: now I need to click ANOTHER time to enter food? I click on a meal and it asks if I want to scan or search, and then it takes me to the screen I wanted to go to - which has scan and search options. Why so many steps to use it???

Love this app. Really powerful. Finally able to track nutrition​exactly. UPDATE 10-18-2025. New Update is very difficult to use. can't simply copy an item to another days meals. you can copy yesterday's meal but not previous days. Not helpful unless you're eating consecutive meals. then, you can't move food from one meal to another. so if I am having leftovers of yesterday's dinner for today's lunch. I can't move the transferred meal from today's ( yesterday's) dinner to today's lunch. 😫

I've been using this app for a couple of months since it was recommended to me by my Dr. But one thing that keeps on happening is that I have an app linked to MyFitness for counting my steps. Now, though, it hasn't been counting my steps, and I've had to install 2 other apps just to make sure that my steps are being counted daily. It's a pain to work out for 45 minutes, and the app didn't count any of my steps during that workout.

EDIT- 1/26 - It's fixed!! I can log in! I'm a little surprised how limited the "log food" option is, it seems limited to SUPER random choices. I can log calories, but no mention of what they're for, so you'll have to go fix your entries after. I'm going up update to 3 stars since it's actually useable, but only minimally. cant even log in on pixel watch, api is BROKEN and issue has persisted for 4+ mo so MFP just doesn't care

Premium+ is NOT worth the money. Meal planning doesn't work well. Need to be able to use DINNER leftovers as LUNCH, not locked into the same meal. Meal plan uses the same side nearly every day. Need to swap sides like mains. . Need to add my own recipes to the meal plan and have MFP build around that. Need shopping lists to port to other shopping apps. Porting to Walmart defaults to frozen/canned over fresh, and the search function does not work to change that.

The numbers just aren't right from screen to screen. If I click view more, only the protein total shows for that category regardless of which category I'm in. Update response: 10/17/25 Why are the copy meal and move item functions no longer available? Those both made use of the app more versatile. I'll stick with my 3 stars and likely switch apps to a different calorie tracking system when my subscription has been completed.

The app is easy to use as long as you are ok logging everything manually. Meal scans mostly don't work, especially for Indian cuisine. Even for easily identifiable dishes, fruits or vegetables it is a hit or miss. The app syncs with Health connect, but doesn't read weight or calories burnt. So I still need to log weight and calories manually in this app. It syncs only the steps and calculates calories based on that, which is much less accurate than something like a smartwatch.

this app has some significant limitations in the amount and detail of data being shared with fitness tracking apps (like Google fit) and that limits the useful information that can be gleaned from the exercise side of things. the food diary is probably the best that's out there but integrations to more online sources of food nutrition data and restaurant nutrition info would help significantly with accuracy.

I want to say the app has no real comparison. That said, There is ZERO reason to charge as much as they do for a monthly subscription. I know development costs and upkeep and all that but damn, most of us are already paycheck to paycheck out here and as good as the app is, having to choose between an app and other bills is ridiculous. You could still make millions charging $9 bucks a month. Quit being Greedy!

It's a decent app, and it works. But it's kinda messed up to make you pay to use the barcode scanner. What really pisses me off is that if you don't pick a meal but still log food, it messes up your diary and screws up your macros. That unlogged meal just disappears, and you can't change or delete it, but it still shows up in your calorie tracker? Seriously, that's so annoying.

I made the move away from MFP officially because of the barcode scanner no longer being free. I have used MFP everytime I have switched from inutitive eating to counting macros in the past 10+ years, always keep it installed on my phone and have loved the interface, but I can't justify needing to pay for something that is free from so many other apps

I downloaded the app and started tracking. when i did the download it wanted financial information and provided an address to cancel within a time but the information disappeared so I can't cancel. then while I was using it it tripled the information for a meal and I couldn't get it fixed. I just deleted the app from my phone. Hopefully I don't get charged!!

Like a lot of other people, I've been using this app for years and it has helped me lose and maintain my weight and fitness. With the newest change making the barcode scanner a premium feature I will be uninstalling the app. The premium features just aren't worth the $80/year price tag. Everything is becoming subscription based and I'm tired of it. For those looking for a free alternative, Fitbit app allows barcode scanning for free (at least for now).

The app is easy enough and the non-pay version serves my purpose well. However, the "Upgrade to Premium" is constantly in your face and cannot be permanently retired. I also don't like the pre-set serving sizes that are sometimes difficult to adjust. Today, I was unable to log anything. App cannot find "Bagel", "Cupcake" or other foods. Wants me to check my spelling. Really?! I'll give it another day to get fixed or uninstall. UPDATE: upgraded to 3 stars for developer response. Time will tell...

I've been using this app for years and absolutely loved the bar scanner. It was so convenient. I'm not moving over to the premium version just for the scanner (the ads to bother me that much). I would leave this app to go to another but this one is the only one that syncs with my health insurance app to get credit for my daily/monthly goals. Shame on you for taking this feature away for us free users.

Been using MFP for a few years and the app has improved a lot. There are a few minor things I would change but overall its great. Edit (08/11/20): For some reason over the last few months when an exercise is added through the automatic sync with my samsung health app, it sometimes adds the exercise multiple times. I will delete the duplicates and eventually it adds it again. This doesn't happen everyday but most days. I've tried to find a way to contact support with no success.

Its the most accurate food logging app I've tried. Tracking your food is hard. Esp when your app can't find what you are eating. MyFitnessPal has at least 90 percent of what I search for, so that part is good. It's user-friendly. That being said, I have been using this app on and off for years now, and the number of features that have disappeared to the premium version is insane. I get certain features being locked, but locking away the barcode option is ridiculous. 19.99/mo is unattainable.

Loving the improvements I've seen over the years returning to this app. Now it's a daily habit. It's great for motivation. *UPDATE- 5 down to 3 stars. No longer as great since they put everything behind a paywall and added a bunch of ads. Also I've used it for 7 yrs straight now and one thing that's always bothered me is that the x-axis for Weight Progress graph when viewing "since starting weight" is that it doesn't show a year, it shows a month and day.

The free version, like all apps, has become slowly enshittified (it's a real thing, look it up.) Basic functionality that used to be free (like scanning barcodes to add food to the diary) is now locked behind a subscription. I can't speak to the experience as a subscriber, but the free version is more like a trial version. I'll be looking for a more accessible free app.

The app has alot of good features and a huge variety of foods to pick from. It has recipes and workouts for beginners and it's great for beginners. I feel like it's too complicated though. Navigating everything and adding certain things like daily body weight and workouts and your own custom foods are too complicated. There are easier to use free aps that are simple and too the point. I find myself using the other app much more. They need to simplify everything.

I've been using the app for a long time, and it's good. Looking things up is pretty easy even after they put the scanner behind a paywall. The weekly views are nice for making sure you hit goals consistently. The reason I don't rate the app higher is because it's buggy. Sometimes, it adds items twice or puts in the wrong servings. Sometimes, it skips ingredients when adding entries from "My Meals" as well. These bugs make it hard to stay accurate with tracking, which is the point of the app.

So I just started using this. Paid for a year up front, since I had never used one before, but didn't want to have to deal with ads. Works great for counting calories and logging fitness. The barcode scanner is the best feature. For how much it charges, someone needs to audit things like calories for certain foods. A check will show one is "official". But what if there are multiple checked entries for the same food? Also linked to my step counter but only counts that toward exercise sometimes.

So far, I like this app. Has had almost every food I have searched and tracks the basics fairly well. UI is OK and easy to pick up the available functions. The few issues I have are minor. For example, I would like to see them add all of the micronutrients and some of the nutritional data is a bit bonkers (1 teaspoon of bacon grease has 3800 calories, for example) and the excercise portion is clunky. Overall it does the basics well and the ads aren't too obnoxious.

Great app that has a wide variety of foods. However, need to make sure checking the right food because sometimes the calories and nutrients aren't right. Update: I decreased my stars. I haven't used the app in a while and decided to get back on it to help me keep track of my foods. Well, now the barcode scanner requires a subscription and I'm not doing that. It used to pair with my Fitbit but now it doesn't. The recipe button is cool though.

Premium+ or Premium is where it's at if you want to get the most out of this app. With Premium+ I can customize my meals a lot easier, with Premium it's honestly such a chore. When you cancel your subscription it cancels it entirely. So if you purchase it for a year, use it for 3 months, then cancel, it'll just drop it all together instead of riding out the rest of the year. Kind of scummy. I wish there was an option to filter out adding in my exercise calories.

Good app to keep track of calories & will help you lose weight if used regularly. But I've a few issues w/ premium version. First, the only thing that makes it worth it is the scan bar code feature. Use to be part of the free version, now behind a paywall because there's no other reason to pay for premium. Second, while I paid for a years premium I'm likely not going to renew. Why? Because ever other time I open the app I'm met with an ad asking to upgrade to premium. Fix this

The app has been malfunctioning for the past few months. Specifically, when I go to the 'Goals' section and try to manipulate my macros, my settings do not get saved and it continually changes back to the default settings. Prior to that, when I would change my goal from, say, maintaining weight to losing a pound per week, the calories wouldn't change (they should have decreased by about 500 calories per day).

I've used this app for a long time, and it helps me when I need to get back on track. I LOVED when they introduced the scan to add food....it was free, then it wasn't. I got super annoyed because they gave that option to me and took it away. The convenience of the scan option, then having to pay for it made uninstall the app. Well now I'm back, still can't afford to pay, just still super annoyed

large catalogue of foods to enter, so it's easy to add foods to the diary. I like getting to see the overall nutrition breakdown and macros of my meals for the day. The app seems technologically stable. I miss some of the features that used to be free (such as scanning barcodes of foods for quick entry). I know everything can't be free because it takes money to run an app, but there are advertisements in the middle of the page so they are getting paid.

I use the free version of this app, and while I find the food lookup functionality to be extremely useful, I think the rest of the basic functionality for counting calories is a little more clunky. Creating a meal assumes you only want to calculate 1 serving, but if you're like me an cooking an entire meal for a family, it would be much easier to count the calories of the entire prepared dish and then indicate how many servings you ate. I also find the constant "go premium" upsell to be annoying

The app used to be a lot better, but there was an update recently and now when I search for foods that I use regularly, it doesn't remember them in my recents anymore, making logging food more time consuming. There has always been an issue with multiple entries having incorrect calorie information (even ones that show the green checkmark). It's also been really buggy lately. I'm not sure if it's just that I've been using it so long and it's not meant to store that much data or what.

Great concept, but lots of usability issues. The app takes a very, very long time to load, doesn't automatically convert foods from search results into my preferred units, doesn't list ingredients so I can see if an item is similar to what I ate, doesn't allow modifications (like no dressing) to search results, and lacks a lot of other obvious improvements that could make it much easier to use. They've added other features few care about instead of improving usability for all.

I used this app mega years ago and recently found my way back to it. I love it! It calculates macros, nutrients, caloric intake/output & has an extensive food database. When I first started using it, I was able to add exercises & build routines. But I recently attempted to add previously built routines & new ones only to find out that everything was lost and I'm not able to build routines - the app shuts down. It worked wonderfully for 2 months then something happened this month! Please fix!

I used to love this app. But now some features that used to be free are premium, and there are so many premium options scattered around that it makes the UI difficult and frustrating to navigate. I wish they'd make like a mfp "light" app like some other companies do, because I'm not paying $20 a month for this. That's over $200 a year. More than a gym membership in some areas!

This app is great for keeping track of calories and exercise. However, the overall user experience is not good in regards to the social support aspect. It seems that the people in charge of the app could care less about the users experience. You won't get regular notifications for sure. Messaging is spotty. The app also differs wildly from the desktop experience. Don't even bother with paying for the premium service. The difference is the premium service will feed you the same number of ads.

The app has one of the largest selection of foods, which is very helpful. Unfortunately the sort on food you have selected before doesn't work and makes finding things you regularly eat very difficult. For example the 'most recent' sort option shows a random list of food eaten in the last few months. And the 'most frequent' sort option shows things that you may have eaten once from several months ago.

App is great, but in it's current state, every time either the ad refreshes or the info on the current page synchronizes, it repositions vertical scrolling location while you're trying to add data. Basically, the page jumps around while you're viewing/adding data. Very frustrating. Also, this app consumes more battery per minute open than any other app I have, including games. Phone gets hot due to rapid battery discharge when the app is open.

Great app, but I miss having the barcode scanner. It used to be included with free users. I just recently had a forced update, and now it wants $80 a year for premium, which is required for the barcode option. Takes longer now to add items and make sure it's a verified product. Hopefully, it will become a free feature again soon.

I used this for years, however, it is expensive and has glitches. The steps don't always transfer in and may not be accurate. Recently, the macros are all wrong. If I click the nutrition tab, that seems accurate. However, if I click on the macros tab to see the pie chart, it's completely wrong including showing carbs instead of net carbs. It's pretty annoying when I'm trying to see quickly where I'm at and maintain being low carb. It might be time to research alternatives.

It is a good app for managing your weight gain (in my case) or loss. One thing that is missing is the ability to ballpark calorie burn for weight lifting. I realize that you can't accurately calculate all exercises, but the big 5 should be listed. Squats, deads, shoulder press, bench and pulling (back work). There are now free apps that have a better interface, are easier to use and are more comprehensive. This will be the last year that I subscribe.

Have only been using this app for about a week but have been very impressed. Looking forward to positive results going forward. Revised 10/2/2019... app has gotten glitchy. First it failed to recognize my food entry and after about 3 years of consecutive entries, I had to start over. Annoying but, oh well. Now, the system stopped clearing out "today", forcing me to log today's food under "tomorrow". Not the end of the world but an unnecessary hassle. Using this since 7/2015, disappointing.

Pros: Love the Food Diary and Database, Love the Calories Counter and Barcode reader. Very useful and simple gives you an idea on what you are eating and how to regulate. Cons: Doesn't work at all with Google Fit, So have to enter everything manually, steps, weight and exercise. Very hard to get customer support and when they do they point out that its not working as of now and tell you to ask again in later date. Not the support that I expected considering it has a subscription based app

Edit: I can add my weight, but it doesn't happen immediately and pressing the button doesn't change the screen at all so it's a guessimg game to see if it went through at all. Cannot add my weight for some reason. Tried to uninstall and reinstall, but did not work. I've been using it for a while and I just encountered this problem today. If it is fixed, I will change my rating.

good app but a few consistency issues and variety of available food options should be reviewed. Ex. I selected American metrics, yes I am constantly getting measurements in grams instead of ounces. I do not eat packaged or pre prepared meals. so many of the dishes I entered have no 'homemade' options leaving me to guess what my calorie count might be. That leads me to another issue. Calorie counts for the same meal vary widely, sometimes by hundreds of calories, how do I choose?

This is a wonderful app and service (check out the website) with one glaring flaw. The food database is huge. Great. Everyone has entered their own modifications to things and then shared them with inconsistent formatting leading to duplicates and unclear entries. That's fine, crowd-sourcing the info is the way to go. But there needs to be a system in place for submitting errors and duplicates. If you need to crowd source moderators, great. But a better curated database is desperately needed.

I use this app a lot because I like how it estimates calories burned for exercise and how easy it is to track some elements of nutrition. I wish selenium, magnesium, b-12, b-6, and zinc were also nutrients automatically tracked with food and teas in this app. I also wish it was possible to change goals for carbs and fats to fit different diets like the ketogenic diet. It's also not fasting friendly--for 5:2 intermittent fasting, it will guilt you for not eating for a day.

Edit: if this was MyNutrtionPal I would have given it higher marks. I expected a fitness app I love the food prtion of this app. I like being able to create a recipe and have the nutrition for it. However, samsung health overall is a much better app for everything else and it is free. From tracking my workouts, to sleep, to tacking my weight (and all the metrics that includes). Even the food tracking is almost as good so I can't justify paying for your app when it lacks so much

This app has many great features, but what I find most difficult is unit conversions. I have a kitchen scale and weigh foods, but too often I'll scan a food and find it won't offer units like grams or ounces. Some foods are too cumbersome to count (chips, nuts, etc). I do not know how to correct foods with bad info. (Example: My popsicle doesn't have 85g of fiber). It nicely syncs with my garmin, and is speedy & efficient. The unit issue will make me quit some day soon, unfortunately.

I like the UI. I wanted to try it for a month. I didn't really care for the multi add feature it was slow and clunky. I also disliked how after adding food it refreshes the entire page. It's slow and frustrating. My goal was to lose weight, but I ended up gaining weight. I only used the verified foods but when I first started logging it seemed like the values were low. I avg 2250cal 220g/p 160g/c 80g/f and. Maybe the creatine made me gain 7 lbs but I'm also very active.

This used to be a 5 star app now because they limit features like barcode scanning unless you pay 79 dollars a year it is not. It's better to start with an app that allows this. Even though this may seem like a small thing it is not. Scanning makes using the app so much better. I get it, if you want to advertise then ok, but limited an important feature doesn't work for me. Especially when I can find it elsewhere.

I loved this app. I've used it for over 10 years and it's helped me lose and maintain my weight in a healthy way. However, the more recent changes where I can no longer scan foods on the free version, being pushed towards the subscription model is a big disappointment. Another money grab by what used to be a great app.

Great app for monitoring calories, fat, sodium, carbs... But it couls be a little more user friendly as far as allowing you to axcess all the charts and percentages without going through a couple menues. Also the price... If it didn't become a monthly subscription to use an app, I would buy it. But for now, I'll use the free version.

Edit-they fixed the bug issues. Works so much better! It's still ridiculous to pay to scan barcodes. Used this app for many years! The app is using my nutritional information from the previous day. When I enter todays food and check my macros, I go back, and nothing is logged anymore! There is a bug issue that needs to be fixed because this is awful!

A program that allows you to keep track of what you eat is in desperate need of updating! Example: when adding food, the quick took option like on the web version, is missing. This makes adding food more difficult since you have to enter everything in again. A search for food for breakfast brings up foods you previously had for lunch or dinner! Somebody please fix this program.

Update 4/4/24: Bar Code Scanner was great when it was free but now all the enjoyable features are part off the paid version. Been looking for an alternative but this is the only thing that works with Garmin Connect unfortunately. Original: Bar code scanner for good is great. Syncs with Garmin Connect (but quickly, and if you modify activities in Garmin they don't update in My Fitness Pal). Overall 4 stars. Like the web app as an alternative.

I really loved this app, and how easy it made it to save and quickly choose previous meals. Whoever decided to push out this latest update needs to be taken out back and shot because it's absolutely abhorrent now. Almost all of the recipes I'd saved are gone, and if I just so happen to decide to go get fast food once in a while, it used to pull up a menu and make picking what I'd ordered easy, now it doesn't even do that. Just remove everything that was recently added in this latest update.

When it works, it works great. What doesn't work is the workouts tracking. One minute, it reads that you did your workouts, next minute, they disappear except your step count and you're left fighting with it to track you're other workouts. It's aggravating and if I didn't need to use it because it's the only connection to a different app I need for my l personal trainer, I would be looking for a different app because I'm tired of fighting with it to track my workouts.

I have been using this app off and on for many years. I'm confused why the scan feature is now premium only. It's also annoying that the app is constantly prompting me to upgrade. I get that is where the money is, but it's super annoying to be prompted after every single thing I log. I'll stick with it for a little longer, but I may just go elsewhere.

Took a break from MFP and just came back. Love the new look, however, the scanner tool was free before. It was the best part of MFP. Wouldn't mind paying for the months I'm going to use the app but both the monthly and yearly price don't make sense. I don't want a year and $20 a month is more insane than Netflix. Downloaded a few newer apps and will probably stay with one of them that is better cost for what you get.

I don't see calorie adjustments for my exercise, including strength training, or my daily steps, even tho I synced the app with my Garmin. Garmin's numbers seem more accurate to me. It's great for calorie tracking, a food diary; wish it better adjusted for exercise. Entered a recipe; app ignored my entered data so info is incorrect and I can't delete it.

Good app - beware subscription renewal. The app itself is good. Good database of food, good functionality. User friendly for the most part. The subscription service is my only issue. I've used MFP for years and done a few of the free trials. Each time I cancelled the subscription within days of starting the trial, and every single time wound up still getting charged for the subscription. Customer service is good enough and always refunds it, but it's shady that this happens so frequently.

Really disappointed that they took the simple barcode scan away. If they take more away, I unfortunately will just simply stop using it. I can't justify the price they want for me to simply log my wt and food. I don't use anything else. I do enjoy this app. it is easy to use and has helped me. I would recommend it to almost anyone. just please bring back the simple bar code scanner.

Edit: I first gave this app five stars because of it's many options, since the latest updates I am removing stars for the shameful greed of the developers. One of the features that has been available to free users since it was introduced was the option to scan food barcodes. That has now been paywalled. Watch them paywall more. Really love this app. It can be used in so many ways. Whether your goal is to improve your nutrition, lose weight, gain weight, or otherwise this app has much to offer.

I am still getting used to using the app. I eat 90% of my meals from emeals. While some can be found, many cannot. I find it annoying to enter all of my ingredients so I cut corners which affect my results. Also the adding exercises is frustrating. I make up my own strength workout that cannot be added to the calorie adjustment and it won't pull anything other than steps from my Fitbit workouts. All in all it is giving me reasonable guesstimate on everything.

2 years later, still nothing done about emphasizing the "PER 100 G". Huge database for food. however a lot are added by users and not verified , thus with mistakes (mainly cause people don't read that the nutritional values are often for 100g while the package or serving size is different. thus causing me to have wrong values even though I entered the right quantity I ate). I also wish the app would give me more tips according to my preferences (ex: vegetarian).

I have used this app longer than I can remember. I paid for premium last year, and it worked with zero issues the entire year. Now that I have opted to forgo the subscription, it won't save or me to delete recipes and glitches and won't reliably search for or log items. I had these issues prior to going premium. I understand not offering premium features for free, but purposely tanking an app to force upgrades says a lot about the company. Maybe don't offer a free app if you can't be honest.

When i first downloaded the app, i liked how i could scan the barcode on a product and it got you all the info. Now, in order to do the barcode scan, i have to subscribe, which i don't want to spend money on something that was free. Besides, i don't want all the other stuff that comes with the subscription. If the barcode was free, this would be a 5 star rating. Otherwise, good app.

Sometimes this app will randomly freeze it enter things multiple times. It has a lot of issues, but overall it works fine. It was surprisingly easy to log workouts. The main problem is that, at times, it will randomly log you out, and once that happens you will be locked out of your account for hours at a time.

Still hasnt figured out tracking with Fitbit (Fitbit isnt innocent in this case either). App is very clunky and can be a pain to use. Wish that the first items that came up in searches for generic foods were servings per weight and not volume. I also wish that there were pictures of the food, either the brand or a regular picture for generics, and also that you could post photos of your own food on yourdiary, like many other apps have. I would much rather use one of the many better calorie counting apps, but I'll give it to MFP that they are the most popular and have the best social media facet.

However, unless your diet consists entirely of packaged food with bar codes, the expectation to know the calorie count of your food is unrealistic. I know that you can create recipes withing the app, but when you get into foods that are more complicated than just scrambled eggs, the last thing you want to do is spend an hour creating a recipe, and that's only the main course. Wait till you get to dessert.

Many of the counts are off. 6 1 oz servings and 1 6 oz serving should be the same amount but they are not. Also the interface is quite confusing and is not intuitive. It becomes tedious to continue tracking daily food intake because every different ingredient has to be tracked separately. Also it would not sync up with Samsung health to track steps on my phone. This app was more trouble than it's worth in my experience.

I really enjoy using the free version of this app. I have been using it for years. Starting October you won't be able to use the barcode scanner for your food anymore unless you buy a subscription. I probably will delete it when that function goes away. Premium has a lot of stuff that they pay for, the scanner should be kept for the free version. Lame.

Bar code scanner now only on Premium. Along with others in the reviews, I'm very unhappy about having to pay $20 mo or $89 a year to use the bar code scanner. I don't care why you moved it to premium. This may be a deal breaker for me. I've used it for years & used to have premium but I didn't feel it was worth it. With so many users upset about the barcode, perhaps MFP should reconsider.

This overall is a pretty great app. Relatively easy to use. the reason I'm giving it 3 stars is because features that used to be standard on this, like the barcode scanner, are now considered "Premium" and now unavailable unless you pay for a membership. I understand that they're trying to make money, but taking away what was a standard feature of the app to try and force people's hand is a douche move

I guess this app is cool. I'll never know cause I had to get rid of it. Everywhere I look,everyone says this app is free, but it never gave me a free option. After I downloaded it and went thru all the setup questions, it showed options for payment. I couldn't see any option for a free version. Not sure if I missed it or not, but that seems like false advertising to me.

The app is like a beta version of a great app. There is so much that it does that very few do. The problem is that a lot of what it does is incomplete. It only tracks the calories from cardio that is entered by creating a workout. The app has no ability to track calorie burn from step counters, none cardio workouts, or from daily steps. Because of this flaw it will always give anybody an incomplete amount of crucial data for aiding them in their health journey.

Well it's been fun but it won't open anymore. I've tried restarting, reinstalling, and all that jazz but I only have a couple days left on my subscription. An app like this is very important but it it doesn't always run smoothly. There are a lot of incorrect food entries but it's easy to add your own. I''ll bump it up to three stars bc MFP is the OG, I haven't used it in a few years it's become more cluttered.

Update: that's a stupid to add to the notes This sucks that you can't track magnesium for your electrolytes. They don't even have the option to add magnesium when you create a food. Their help site says to create a food and add your supplements there. Hello!!! It's not listed. I mean how hard is it to add it to the list of nutrients. That post was a few years ago and it's still not an option. They have no problem changing the app for the worse. I have premium. I expect to be able to custom add.

I had high hopes for this app, but sadly it is extremely limited. The food database is good, but the tracking is very clunky. The workouts are limited and have a fee for basic functionality. Actually, everything useful is locked behind a paywall and isn't done in a way that makes it appear worth the cost. The bones are there, but this just us not it.

Kind of sucks tbh. I can't edit anything. There is no way to change an entry if you make a mistake. I logged dinner after midnight and the app posted this dinner to the next evening instead of the dinner earlier in the day. There is no way to change or fix this. I'm giving this app until the end of the week then I am deleting it. I need it to work around my schedule. Update: Email sent but it's not a bug. It's poor design. Update: I can't add my fitness tracker either.

While I have been using the app since 2013, the latest update has ruined the functionality of the app. Previously, I could type a brand name into the search and pull up recent items. Now I have to type in a keyword from the item description. Bring back the ability to search by brand! It also deleted all my recent foods and I now have to re-enter them. An update should make the app better, not break previous functionality!

This used to be a great app, but the most recent update is a major step back. Meals you save and then use on another day used to put the exact foods, so you could easily customize amounts. Now the meal gets added as a single item Search algorithm got a huge downgrade. You used to be able to search by maker and no longer can. Searching Starbucks in my recents doesn't bring up my Starbucks drinks, I now need to type the specific drink

I'm back again to BEG you to give us the option to use the old interface! I'm a premium subscriber who loved being able to flip between dates, see full meals, etc. Now? Gone. I'm also no longer able to re-complete a diary if I add a food item unexpectedly-what's up with that?! And a longtime gripe I've had is that users shouldn't be able to enter any food item that's not homemade without entering the FULL nutrition label info. Please, this app has been a lifesaver for me--fix it!!

This app is overall good. It would be better if there were other options for entering food, like scanning barcodes or taking a picture without having the premium subscription. Also, recently the weight estimate based on your day's meals and calories has changed. It was once very accurate, but now the estimate is way below a reasonable weight loss amount based on that day's meals and activities. Please correct this!

This app has a few features I need that some others don't, but that advantage is ruined by its poor useability and reliability. Inefficient UI. Lots of garbage in the database. I have to restart the app several times a day when it starts getting slow to respond. The water intake log often forgets entries after initially appearing to accept them. Lately it's been forgetting food entries, too, or placing them on the wrong date. Stops showing my food history in search results. The list goes on.

This app could be really good and worth paying for, but currently it's buggy and inconvenient. I can't view or copy from friend's diaries, either on the app or the website, and it shows my calorie goals instead of theirs when trying to view their diary. Adding meals is ok, but when adding recipes you can't scroll the ingredient list when it gets too long and runs off the page. You also can't add recipe weights, which is a pain when measuring servings.

This app was great when I used it years ago. Now? It seems functionality is all locked behind a paywall. My chief complaints: 1. the app is super slow and buggy. Entries often don't sync between the app and desktop versions. The recipe import function is so convenient, but editing the imported recipes is such a headache due to the glitches. 2. the food library is wildly inaccurate. one food item from a specific brand will have pages and pages of results, all with different calories.

2026: App has had major issues for 45+ days. It's listed as a known issue but meanwhile the app isn't saving progress photos since January 30th, and exported CSV data is missing bunch of data points, etc. I would reinstall to see if that fixes it, but their webpage says not to do that until they issue a fix... 2015: Super easy to use. Remembers recipes, eating habits, everything. Have previously lost 60 lbs using it. Back at it to drop some of the weight I put back on since I stopped using it.

This used to be a wonderful app. I cannot get this app to read Health Connect for accurate exercise or steps. The log for food is okay. and I like being able to input the recipes for calorie counting. I paid the subscription. what a waste of money. As of 03/22/2026, this is still a major issue No matter how.many times I sync, it stops recording steps.

I am terribly disappointed with the most recent "upgrade" and I will not be renewing my paid subscription. I target calories, carbs and protein BY MEAL and was very satisfied with how the prior version displayed these. The new user interface strips away the immediate data and forces me to click around to get data I need, which previously was visible at first glance. Other changes also create more steps for user. It had bern ideal for years! Disappointed.

Not sure what's happened in the last few weeks, but the application won't open on my phone anymore. Stays frozen on the loading screen. I've uninstalled and reinstalled the application multiple times, restarted my phone, and checked for OS updates on my device with no progress. Would gladly rate it 5 stars if it worked for me again!

How do you have a known bug, one that was created by an upgrade, for over a month? Been using MFP since 2011. always has been good but the issue of not synching with the website is a big deal. Logging on the phone is simple but I also need to log on the website because I'm sharing with others. I end up having to manually log a few days there at a time. Not convenient and it doesn't help that logging on the website doesn't overwrite what was entered in the app but duplicates it.

The paid version is worth it - at least for me. This app is extremely reliable and helpful for tracking different health metrics. I love that it connects easily to my Garmin and Fitbit apps so I don't have to enter data in multiple places. Edit: Now 2 from a previous 5 star review. This new interface is absolutely unusable. Go back to the old version. Too clicks to log something or get to the data you want to see. I like the quick log of previous meals, but this could've been added elsewhere.

I pay for the premium version of the app. unfortunately I paid for the year in advance to save some money. After paying I found there are several "known issues" with the Android version. First it doesn't sync to the web so all the reports and statistics in the web version are unavailable to me, a paying premium member. My spouse (who also paid for a membership) cannot see my diary so it makes logging the food more difficult. Lastly it is not syncing exercise with Fitbit. Definitely pay for it.

Unless they fix the 0 calorie bug, this is useless for me. I had high hopes with how easy the interface was, but I had several items that showed the calorie count before logging come in with 0 calories in the diary. How am I supposed to cut if my calorie intake is severely underreported. I'm on another app now that actually works. Uninstalling this one.

My entires keep disappearing!! After I log exercise, it acknowledges it by increasing my available calories, then 2 hours later I'll log on and the exercise and additional calories are gone. It does the same when I log meals. they show up, calories are adjusted, then it dissappears and defaults back, over and over. the app is super easy to use. it's a shame that it keeps doing this.

Great for tracking food, but the past few weeks hasn't synced between the app and the website, which means it doesnt update with any other apps synced. Tech teams advice: just update on the website. I dont have the time to sit down and double log all my meals, why not just fix a repeatedly raised issue?? Plus it constantly pushes for me to get premium despite all the issues.

The new update is miserable. Can I get the old view back? It's nice to see the daily overview up-top, but I can no longer view everything I've logged per meal at a quick glance. And as far as logging a meal, it is not as simple as it was. I have paid for premium for a long time and have used this app for years, but this update is my least favorite and might be the first time I'm already considering finding a new app for tracking.

I've been using this app for the last 2 years but since Jan, it has stopped showing my friends data and vice versa - mine doesn't show up for him. my friend lives in my house & eats the same meals I do, so being able to share the meals back and forth is what makes the app useful. we've tried all the troubleshooting things including syncing, restarting device & app, Uninstalling the app etc and it still is not working.

I'm no longer able to save meals in this app. I added a meal yesterday, received confirmation that the meal was added successfully. Then today I went to add the same meal to my food diary and it's vanished. So I created the meal again, received the same confirmation that it was created. Go back to Meals to add it to food diary, it doesn't exist. Please fix this bug!

Doesn't track weight accurately. that's the only thing I use this app form I've tracked my weight in it for about 15 years, and I liked the XY scatter plot graph. now the graph is inaccurate and does not show all weights in the weekly and monthly views. it gives a general idea in the since starting view, but its still not accurate. Time to find a better weight app

6/18/23: Great app, but currently, I cannot get MFP to sync with Fitbit and/or Google fitness to import my steps. I disconnected and reconnected to them, but no such luck. I also wish that they didn't take the barcode scanning ability out of the free version. 1/12/26: I just subscribed to the premium version and find I'm having the exact same issues I described above a couple years ago! Very disappointed and hope that I can get it resolved.

Ok, but kinda hard to use without premium pay. On opening there is an ad which makes it hard to use quickly. Quick add log only allows calories and no labels/name. I don't see the point of paying for something that locks basic qol features behind a paywall. Will likely switch to a different logging app soon based on my experience. It gets the job done, but an excell sheet provides almost the same value and has no ads.

This app is so, so buggy. It's unfortunate because it could be a great app, I really like the meal scan and voice logging. The integration with Google HealthConnect is broken. The exercises from my watch are consistently duplicated in MFP, sometimes up to 10 times. This means a 200 kcal training shows up as 2000 kcal, i.e. this app can't be used to count calories accurately (its whole purpose). Same thing for voice logging, meals get added twice and you need to manually delete them.

When it works, it's great... emphasis on WHEN. randomly a few days ago, the app stopped accessing the food database and only gave me my food history in the search results. What was even weirder too, was sometimes when I finished typing the food, it would show up for a half second then disappear. I tried uninstalling and reinstalling the app, signing in and out, clearing the cache and app data but nothing fixed the problem, and it still doesn't work to this day. IM DONE

The new diary view, hiding most of the entries, is really annoying, not an improvement in the slightest. Way too many steps to review my log for the day and add any missing entries. Much too difficult to get to the progress graph. Also, despite all the changes, logging foods still double-enters some items, seemingly at random — and now with the contents of each meal hidden by default, it's harder to notice.

From 5 stars to just 2! Why did you change the app so drastically? It was perfect before you started messing with it! The weight and step tracking was right up front and easy to access. Now I have to search around and there is no history or photos. Logging meals is completely tedious with extra steps added to the logging. All of the things that made this app simple and effective to use are gone. I will be looking for an alternative and not renewing my premium membership going forward.

Update: 10/2025 I really dislike most of the the changes made on the last update. Logging food and viewing and nutrition is not intuitive especially after years of the old format. Original:Great app. I lost 30 plus pounds. Found a number of good recipes. Syncs with Garmin. I'm back found part of the lost pounds. The issue with Samsung seems to be resolved. Wish they would add the option to track glucose and blood pressure.

It's okay. Kinda a cash grab app. You get access to recipes for free but not the simple stuff you have to pay for like scanning food to keep track, and a bunch of other stuff. You can't keep track of different sets of workouts, like I'm doing sets of bench presses with a set of weights, then I increase the weights and sets and won't it wont let me keep track of that? It doesn't update quickly with my Garmin watch. Carb Manager is a better app to keep track of food and workouts but no free recipe

The meal planner is unconscionably bad, especially given that it's a paid feature. You're unable to log from their premade recipe book, current meal plans get archived (meaning you can't log meals from THOSE either) once you start planning for the upcoming week, you can't add your own recipes to your plan, and the calculator seems to not even try to stick to your macros or calories. I really wanted to like this feature, but its execution is horrific.

loved this app forever. Pro user. The voice log feature is amazing. But now it's basically quit syncing across devices (I have two phones I use daily) and I have to log back in several times a week. Please fix and I'll update to 5 stars. Other issue - you can't choose which Google account you want to use to login. It just picks one. I have several on this device.

latest update has a lots of "bugs/ glitches" to fix, Oct 2025. I'm 9 mo.s into my first paid year. i only had a couple of complaints with previous version, & I know that some of the changes are actually enhancements now ... BUT not having today's date in each view doesn't feel like "tracking". I mean if I'm going to keep paying for a service, wouldn't i expect it to get easier when it changes? (I'd tell you how many clicks it takes to get my water intake updated but I can't count that high).

This app is awesome! I love being able to track my meals and see nutrition content such as carbs, sugar, proteins, etc. It also gives you articles in the newsfeed about fitness, meal prep, and other things that may peak your interest. The contests are very engaging. Even if I never seem to win, I'm motivated to complete the contest challenges! 12/29/2025 Update: This used to be an amazing app with a lot of great features included. Now they charge a fee for the previously free features. Smh.

Have used this since 2012. premium for 10 years. It used to be awesome. whatever you did this year has damaged all the terrific features I came to love. I ate the same breakfast bowl twice a week for years. suddenly, you can't find it even by searching. also, the time of the food I enter constantly resets itself instead of using the time I just entered on the last item. I tried to find a way to send a complaint but couldn't find address for company. please restore the prior excellent features.

I've been using the (premium) app for quite a while for food logging, but it's horribly inconsistent. I mean I eat the same foods 3 days in a row, and I have to SEARCH for it every time I have to make an entry? I do like the Meal Planning options but food logging gets a bit ridiculous with this interface. Luckily I get it for free through work because I wouldn't pay for it.

Upgrading to the premium version of this app was a big mistake. I recently cannot find 90% of my foods I've used before, I am constantly having to log back into the app because for some reason it keeps logging me out. None of this ever happened when I had the free version. What is going on? I see so many bad reviews now. I never had a single issue with this app until I upgraded to Premium. Why am I paying for problems now? If this doesn't resolve I'm going to have to find a new nutrition app.

What happened to you, MFP? I downloaded this app again after several years, and it has become a shameless cash grab of nonsense. what do you mean I have to pay to use the barcode scan feature now? every time I open the app I have to close out the ad for premium. premium prices for features that used to be basic. shameful. the ads make it so incredibly annoying. and if it were a reasonable price, maybe it wouldn't be quite so ridiculous.

Changed from 5 to 2 bc every time I get on the app it asks if I want to upgrade to premium. I don't. Remembering to do track my cals is a challenge (I've been successful but it is a new habit) and every 3 days MFP logs me out. I'm assuming it's bc I don't have premium. This app has worked for me in the past and I'm used to the interface, so I'm not going to delete it, but if you are new to cal counting, pick a different app unless you want to be accosted for 60 dollars on the daily.

I used this app a bunch years ago. came back this week and it is terrible. what happened? I add things and it dou le or triple adds them. the app freezes and shows a blank screen when I log a meal. and in between all of that they nag me to subscribe. 5 to 10 years ago I would have been first in line to pay for some functionality but the basics don't even work so why would I fork over $$. need to find an alternative.

Totally Frustrating that the company decided to take all the great features, like scanning and photos, that help people succeed with their goals and force then to pay exorbitant fees to access them. I keep the free version but only use it because my years of weight loss history is on here. I found another daily log that doesn't charge so much to use the scan features.

The scanner for barcodes is great and the record streak is cool! No detailed reports on a daily to yearly history similar to Samsung health is a huge gap in service. Linked devices stopped after working for 6 months. The app is not user friendly and the thing that annoys me the most is constantly having to login. Especially because this app is blocking my password autofill.

Android Version is atrocious. Recent foods do not populate. Foods that I eat frequently. I start typing and it does not show up. Barcode scans take multiple tries before recognizing. New user interface is less user friendly. Apple version has more options to input snacks. Just all around not great. Incredibly frustrating.

takes too long to add calories. for 3 consecutive days I have logged the meals, and every time it takes 5-10 minutes (sometimes more) to see the total. I think it is X and it shows X + 300, or even 500, only after the day is over. I cannot accurately check my totals in hindsight. very frustrating.

I used to love this app for it being efficient and user-friendly. Taking out your phone and snapping a photo of the barcode used to be easy. Now if I choose to have breakfast for dinner, I have to go rescan those items in the dinner section. Makes me not even want to open the app to scan, since I have be by the fridge to record my meals. Not a smart update.

I use another app. I got a Garmin watch and seeing that they are partnered app., I started using MFP. I am not overly impressed. 1. The app I use puts your daily activity on one screen and focuses on Calorie intake and medicine intake. If you want other stuff it is a click away. (graphs charts) I feel MyFitnessPal trys to put too much stuff on the intro page. 2. No medicine tracker. 3. Even though it pairs with Garmin it really does very little but roll up a Calorie count.

Update Oct 2025, is the worst now, why doesn't MyFitnessPal read and pay attention to all of their Customers dissatisfied reviews on the update. Not intuitive meaning no longer an easy user interface, Lags big time, hard to navigate, does not save our Foods any more, several of my foods are no longer in my profile list when I want to add them, sometimes kicks you out of app. Really terrible, change it back please! Again listen to users comments.

Been using this app for years prior to the addition of "premium" & "premium plus" The constant ads & prompts to pay is annoying. The price too high. Was aggravating when I opened the app to find out I was no longer able to do certain things I was able to do prior when the "premium" addition was added.

I used to love this app until they started charging for it way back when. Today I thought I would try again &. downloaded app. Nope it is worse than before. Can't use basic calories counts. They are only allowing percentages. I tried to enter a meal & it would only give me food lists from the food lists I used way back in 2017ish. I eat a lot differently than I used to. I am so disappointed in what used to be a wonderful program.

Update: Once again the search all function isn't working when trying to log food. Tells me there are no results when I try to search for anything. Highly annoying. All of a sudden I can't do a search all for foods. When I type something in, no foods load. It tells me no results found. I logged out,logged back in. Even uninstalled the app and reinstalled it. Still won't populate any foods when using search. Very disappointed!

I can't understand why developers feel like they HAVE to change things around when the app already worked fine. Now it takes me twice as long to log my meals because if I log the meal well after I ate it and forget to adjust the time it will log all the items at the time of writing then I need to go in and change every single item manually. Not worth it. Most of all the app is not really helping me to lose weight. Don't think I'll renew the premium membership.

I've been using the app for years, but the new layout is terrible. The old layout allowed for a quick review of foods logged and a summary of calories at the top (with the base target calories and exercise credited calories clearly separated). The new layout requires entering the meal view to see what has been logged and only made things easier if you eat the extact same meal every day. The new design is unnecessarily bulky, you can only see a few logged items on the screen without scrolling.

I can't understand how you want me to purchase premium when the basic version is garbage. It says"food logged" and it may or may not have actually logged it. Then I can only see how many foods I've logged. No details, or which ones actually got logged. No removing something you accidentally logged. Definitely needs to be more user friendly

You need premium to scan food bar codes when there's other apps that scan it for free. Only reason I use this app is because it has my starting weight saved from 5 years ago when this used to be the best app. Edit: now there are a ton of ads. I moved completely to another tracking app, I cannot stands the ad between the Dinner and Snack sections. its so obnoxious. Edit 2: When I first got this app YEARS ago, it didn't have ads interfering with the user interface.

great tracking for your food. I wish it had better integration for tracking weightlifting. I will see how it does as I incorporate a smart watch. Update: The removal of features on the free version and moving them to paid has made this a burden to use. On top of removing features I had been using, its also plagued with pop ups that happen while youre actively typing. I am now deleting this and moving to another app

the free version is decent, but every thing you do, it asks you to upgrade. it eventually stopped showing my macros (protein etc) which is the only reason I was even using it. i actually WOULD have upgraded if it was reasonable but $80/year is expensive. Samsung health has a great calorie tracker that is free abd has a lot of the premium features (barcode scanning etc). deleting this app, bc developers are greedy.

Every update is worse than the last. Been using the app (paid subscription) for years. 10/2025 update worst yet. The new interface is horrible. So many extra steps for everything. Can't swipe to previous /next day. Can't see all the foods logged for each meal unless you click on the time of day. Still doesn't let me save my created meals correctly. Thinking about changing calorie counting apps. So many out there. Plus the different subscription tiers now is kind of ridiculous.

the most recent update ruined an excellent app. whoever designed the interface never used the app. Having to click and open Breakfast to see what you ate are too many clicks. Also the update is so much slower to add food. As a paying customer for years, they need to add an option to go back to the old Interface. If they don't I will no longer subscribe. please fix!

Ad-filled to the point of being unusable. The premium subscription is outrageously expensive and they know it - so they pester you with nonstop, FOMO discount offers to the point of driving you insane. That's bad enough without the app also just being a pain to use. There are better alternatives, trust me. Do some research and avoid MyFitnessPal.

I dropped this app before because it took away barcode scan in the free app. I downloaded it again because the Google search said that the feature was available on free. At 1st I am sure I could use it but today again, I have to buy a plan to use. I like the app, but besides the slightly larger food database, it is not much different than other apps that have the feature, so I will delete it again, I guess, and download another.

I've used this app on and off for the last couple of years and it was very handy. One of the most convenient features in the free version WAS the barcode scanner. Now you can only access by going to the premium version. They keep saying "you can still look everything up", but you get a million entries with varying information (even on what looks like the exact same it). Guess I'll see how long before I get too frustrated and find a new app.

I've used the app for over a decade. The calorie tracking is top tier. But lately, the app has been laggy and unresponsive making it painfully slow to add foods. The data sync to Google fit, Samsung, etc randomly breaks all the time, making it almost impossible to consistently use with a wearable. The update looks a lot more polished, but it seems it broke a lot of the core functionality Update: cleared data and uninstalled to see if it fixes the lag and now I can't even log in.

I used the app for years and the barcode functions made it easier to use, especially when you can't find the food items in just a search. It's too much of a hassle to continuously search for items that are hit or miss could be easier to scan in a second, but to pay for a function all of a sudden seems ridiculous. And the premium prices is too high.

It's a neat app, I really like the idea of it. I appreciate the barcode scanner and the ability to track meals and exercises. However, half the time my searches don't work. Pages don't load, even while connected to wifi. I also can't upload photos or complete my food diary due to in app errors. It's too frustrating to use regularly, so I'm discontinuing use of the app.

Constantly switches screens on you (without your consent). Half of the screen is usually covered by crud & adds. Interface is very cumbersome. 2 stars for excellent sync, and for search by bar code which is outstanding (this is the only thing I use it for: to look up nutrition facts sometimes). Daily, I use another app to keep track of my diet.

It was a better app in 2012 (5 stars) than 2020 (2 stars) eight years later. I've used both the free and premium versions. The free version has too many ads & links, it makes it difficult to navigate now. The premium version is too expensive considering the lack of added value. Yes, it has more bells & whistles and less ads, but it doesn't increase the overall experience enough to merit the cost.
