For 8+ years, we have been asking for a space for sleep tracking along side exercise and water.

Used this app off and on for ages. Whenever I felt myself slipping too hard I always started back up to track. After they put the barcode scanner behind a pay wall (it had been available for ever) I lost all willingness to use it as more than just a weight tracker, which other apps are more than capable of doing without all the extra. Time to find something new. No 😃 the feature was a convenience thing, that was available for years, and you put it behind a pay wall. Kick rocks.

I had given 4/5 stars years ago. The app was great and was an easy way to set goals and keep on track. I just installed it again after a couple of hiatus, and it's full of ads, constant pops about premium, and the homescreen is condensed and messy. The barcode scanner used to be free, and the app seems bogged down! I suppose it still offers some decent features, but it isn't worth the annoyance. Now, a 2/5 star and uninstalled again.

Used this app for years, and it was fantastic for what I needed, until they decided to put most of the useful features behind a pay wall. Yeah, you can manually track stuff, but one of the most useful features was the barcode scan, which now is only usable for subscribers. The price is insanely high for what features they do offer, and if you don't sign up, you're constantly berated with ads to join premium. Luckily other apps exist with those useful features still available free.

This app has been glitchy for a while. I always have to check that it didn't add the same food two or three times. Sometimes I delete something and then it reappears a second later. Now it's even worse. Duplication happens daily, and it very often does not show my goals under the nutrition summary. I have been logging my food on this app every day for more than 3 years, and I'm used to it, but I'm considering switching because this app has gotten so buggy. And yes, I have the most recent update.

I used to live this ap. However, the accuracy has gone down hill. When the nutritional information doesn't match up (not even close) it's not very useful. Also, adds are annoyingly places front and center rather than at the top of bottom. The screens are too full and finding or reading information gets difficult. I could purchase and go add free but with the accuracy issue it doesn't make sense to do so. Also, it is too expensive for me.

Very disappointed with app now days. Features and usability was much better a few years ago. Some options that used to be free are now hidden behind a pay wall. About $20 a month or $80 a year (with current special) is more than I'm willing to pay to see if I get functions I had before. Map my walk no longer syncs well with it. (Yes I have the current edition of both) It's become very unsuitable for my purposes Seriously considering finding something else. :( Thanks a lot for the "upgrades"!

Not what it once was... Oh the joys of yesterday are gone with price increases and companies changing things up on ya. I took a small hiatus from the app due to getting a very time demanding job, and I'm coming back to find many of the features, simple ones like scanning barcodes, are paid for feature. Ugh... So much for ease of use. I don't see this lasting very long and will look for another app that doesn't want to nickel and dime my usage.

I need 3 things: track macros, plan meals ahead and log unexpected meals/food. Tracking and logging is great. I saw some old docs showing the meal planner and it seemed really cool, but whatever they did to "revamp it" really messed it up. It's super confusing and basically unusable. I know have to spend an entire month being "educated" on how to plan meals? This is dumb.

This app has really gone to $h!t. You used to be able to scan a barcode to find the exact match (99% of the time), now you have to pay for that feature. You used to be able to copy a meal from the day before, now it just pulls up a random meal from a week ago with no ability to choose a different meal or a different day. This is very annoying when you have to add several entries for a meal that has a lot of ingredients. More and more features are being taken away and held for ransom. 2/5 str

Most useful tools are locked unless you pay. The "diary" for adding your food is horrendous. Once you create a meal, you can't change or delete it. If you try to look for food, there are thousands to look through and 90% of them were entered wrong. So if you add another, it just makes the list longer. Thousands of the same exact item, just spelled differently. Changes are not saved or randomly doubles entries. You would think that there would be a lot more options to narrow down the food list.

Great app, but the corporate side got them by the balls, and it isn't what it used to be unless you pay an absurd amount. I can live with ads, but making things like the barcode scanner a paid feature when it always was free in the past is so absurd and sides with "money-grabbing" instead of showing genuine attempts at QOL for users. IMO, in its current state, you have better free options out there or with at least far more reasonable prices.

I used to use this app, and it was great. I wasn't able to use all of the features, because I didn't have the right kind of smart watch, but loved that I could quickly scan bar codes to enter new foods. I recently decided to start tracking my calories again and updated the app and can now only use the barcode scanner if I purchase a subscription. I get that prices on everything are going up, but I guarantee I'm not the only person who ditched the app over this.

I really like the app! However, it is super frustrating that my exercise is not counted in my daily diary. My Samsung Health app is connected and shows up but when I go to complete diary for the day, it disappears. I have tried restarting the app, clearing the cache, and uninstalling the app but nothing works. I was considering purchasing the premium version but decided against since I can't get the free version to work correctly.

I don't know whats going on with this app. I used it this morning and now all of a sudden it logged me out and won't recognized my password or email. I've reset it several times and still am not able to log-in, very frustrating. Please fix the problem, this app was amazing until now. I will resubmit my review when the problem is fixed. Edit: Thank you guys for fixing the app in such a timely manner. It was a little buggy upon first log-in, but the fact that I can log-in is definitely an upgrade.

Through the years MyFitnessPal has gone from a light calorie tracking diary to a bloated ad riddled mess. It's a shame, the core features are nice. They have a huge database of foods, such that I have never had to manually enter a food. It syncs quickly with my smartwatch. Unfortunately that's all terrible to use now. The UI is more cluttered than my grandma's basement and plastered with ads. I cannot recommend MFP anymore.

Buggy. How hard is it to keep my data in the mobile app and web version in synch? App duplicates or deletes entries randomly. Changes what screen I'm on according to its fancies. Doesn't default to my preferred measurement units. Fails at all the basic things even a newbie developer would get right. Food database is OK, but is often out of date. Many foods I eat regularly have been wrong for a year. I have no idea how they sell premium versions if the free one is so unreliable.

Features are helpful but nutritional information, even on "verified" items it FREQUENTLY incomplete. You can suggest a correction but can't log it as you corrected it; instead, you have to create a whole new item. Most errors are related to sugar alcohol and net carbs. Also, they are unresponsive when you enter corrections, there is no way to know if they are implemented.

Edited April 2021: Not good anymore. Unfortunatley uninstalling and dropping premium because the support sucks. Can't use this for what I need it for anymore and not going to pay $70 a year for something broken. Switched to "Lose it!" App. I use this app all the time. It's easy to use and it makes it easy to find food. It's helped me lose 30 pounds, I wouldn't have been able to do it without it.

I've used this app religiously since June 2021, I stayed even after they put the scan feature behind a pay wall. Lately, when I add food, it adds it twice, and if I don't catch it, it makes me think I have fewer calories when I do. Next is the food marked green that are supposed to be "correct." I'm going by the label on the food, but the app shows anywhere from a ÷/- in calories & carbohydrates. I know my tracking is not always 100% accurate, but how can they allow incorrect info?

I used this app for a really long time and it was super helpful. Great catalog of foods that could easily scan the barcode and go about your day. Unfortunately it has changed to where you must pay to be able to scan the barcodes to get the nutritional facts. So I am uninstalling and hunting on. If you are willing to pay for the better features of the app it will be the right app for you.

I had used this app a few years ago. I've recently restarted a fitness program and decided this app would be helpful again. Alas, I've already noticed that two of the most useful features are now for paid premium users: the barcode scan and the calorie breakdown of macro nutrients. I was pleased that when I logged in, I still had access to the recipes I had loaded in previously. That one redeeming quality may keep me on board for a while. I'd rather switch to another app than pay mega $$$.

I've used this app for over a decade, for better or worse. Now it's impossible to add physical exercise. Add that to the slow creep of intrusive video ads, buggy "recent" meals that don't show up properly, the reminder notifications that have NEVER worked consistently and you've got a textbook example of tech ensh!tification. All this needed to be was a database to help people who were actually trying to be healthier. Great job UA 👎

I've used this for years and now you have to pay for the bar code scanner?? What come on... I get ok but $80 pet yr... no way. Other apps with similar features are at most $25 for the yr. Your charging $20 per month that's double the other is my gym membership. I will track it on my notepad on my phone instead before I pay $80. I don't need the order features never used them and not interested in them to be honest.

Now more confusing and way too busy. Wow. I haven't used MFP since before I was pregnant. Just downloaded it back to discover it's now unnecessarily challenging to find the food you logged. And all the stuff that used to be free is now behind a paywall....not the barcode scan :,(! It barely fulfills a purpose for me anymore. Actively looking for a better app. I just wish this one was like it used to be with its cleaner design and user friendly options.

This was my first 5 star review-Idk where I would be without this app, not only does it help me track my calorie intake but it also helps me keep track of my macros! :) Updated review- Very disappointed that they took away the free barcode scanner, made everything faster and easier as I'm always on the go and don't have time to search for my items. App has been very glitchy and frustrating. Not going to pay for the subscription so looks like I'll be looking for a new app to use unfortunately..

This is the least intuitive app I've ever had to deal with. It won't let you unlink fitbit accounts. The disconnect feature just doesn't work for that one. An error appears if you try to delete your account and it will just log you out. If you try to create a new account it will not accept any emails (even if they've never been used). I've heard good things about tracking food through the app but everything else is garbage and you can't even create a new account, so what's the point?

After I had my second child I decided to reinstall this app because I remember how easy it was to use. And using the bar code scanner was great because it allowed me to find the right item and it just saved time. Now that you have to pay for the feature, it is pretty disappointing. It kinds makes this app less appealing. Unfortunately I will be deleting the app.

Used to love it. stores what i eat for quick use. But once it was sold to another company, they're really pushing premium mode. Ads are getting more obnoxious and annoying and you can no longer use scan mode unless you go premium. You can still manually search for foods but I'm guessing it's a matter of time before that feature is removed for non premium users to try and force you to upgrade. In short, now it's just okay but leaning towards terrible.

This app used to be very very good, but then they got greedy and started wanting more money for the same features that were free. Such as the barcode scanner, now you have to type in everything you eat and you have to type in every type of exercise you do individually which takes up time. In order to use the barcode scanner you must pay $79.99 a year or $19.99 per month. Horrible experience.

For the most part its handy. But the are alot of programming problems that make it a pain in the ass to set it up and log things. Bar code read is a nice feature, but is wrong most times. Then the app wont let you modify the serving size fat , protein, etc. to get it match the label. Makes you do it all manually. Takes foreverWhen trying to make a "meal" and I bar code read or enter info manually, ya hit the check mark to accept it as a line item in your meal, but it won't log it. Frustrating!

Fitness Pal is a good app,and can help ppl lose weight, I just discovered I have to go premium to use the barcode scanner I've been using free for years, if you would have sent a email making us aware of this change maybe we wouldnt be so upset, although most of my meals/ foods items have been scanned and are in the app. I understand a new corporation bought out Fitness Pal but charging more isn't right! Like my husband and son I'm going to try Lose It App, it has the scanner and so much more ❤

Useful, but glitciher and glitchier. I can't imagine paying money. I've been using this app for 10+ years and appreciate many aspects of it, especially the ability to add my own recipes. In that time, several updates have been released only to disappear behind a paywall. Annoying, but the recent (months long) issue where the app adds extra instances of foods all over your diary whenever you change anything is almost the last straw. If it weren't for all my stored recipes, I would switch.

Terrible, terrible design. User interface is the most confusing, non-intuitive design imaginable. Also, wildly inconsistent calorie counts for food items. I typed in 1 cup cooked quinoa and saw search results ranging from 120 calories to 225. The search results appeared crowd sourced, so accuracy is dubious. People just write in their opinions, defeats the purpose of counting calories. Stay away from this app.

Edited Rating to 1-star. App is pretty sub-par these days. Does not sync with my Fitbit app to transfer calories burned and steps. This doesn't work regardless of syncing is done via HealthConnect (updates sporadically and randomly) or directly with Fitbit. The entire platform is becoming useless if it cannot track the most basic and relevant metrics. Disappointing as a paying subscriber. Will cancel if it isn't resolved satisfactorily.

What Am I paying for, if every time I do a search for something I ate within the last day doesn't show up? This is annoying and time consuming. There really isn't much of an advantage to paying for a premium membership if I have to go through all the extra work to find the foods that I'm commonly eating anyway. Perhaps it's an algorithm change to get people to pay for the premium plus. Regardless, I likely will not be renewing my membership.

I've had this app a very long time. It's extremely frustrating. It glitches out frequently and is slow. It is the ONLY app that does this. Their solution is to empty out the cache. I'd be doing that multiple times a day. They know it's an issue but have done absolutely nothing for their consumers. I'd switch to another one but I have everything saved from years of use. Then we have premium. Why would I pay for this if the above problems have never been fixed. Internet is not slow. Do better!!!

I have had this APP for years. My subscription was coming due. Thankfully, my credit card company declined the charge. When I checked where it came from it was from MFP. It was $30 dollars more than the amount they had been notifying me of. Their subscription has skyrocketed over the years and they just hit my limit! Trying to slip it through my credit card on the next level of annual subscription is really what caused me to cancel. Beware.

Why would I ever pay for a premium membership on this app if the free version is this buggy already. Friend requests feature is malfunctioning. It was only after I added them through my phone contacts that it finally worked. Now, the app won't sync with the desktop version for the food diary. Meaning each time I logout of the app, all progress is lost. No use in re-entering data, still won't sync. Support says to use the desktop version for now, completely defeats the purpose of having an app.

App has not been syncing (uploading) with their website since late January, Therefore not syncing with Fitbit. I've already seen that this is a known issue, lots of comments in the community. Their response is to enter your food diary on their website. I don't always have my laptop with me and that page does not work well on my phone, too small to read. This is absurd that it's gone on for 2 months.

The app does as advertised but I won't give it more than 1 star until they fix the sync issue. New phone season is here and everyone who is upgrading will be stuck losing any unsynced data or, like me, be forced to use my old device to track calories until they fix the problem. It has been an issue for months now and it's honestly unacceptable. I'll be finding an alternative app if it isn't corrected soon.

If I could rate negative I would. Not a user friendly app. not useful as a free app, wouldn't allow a free trial, and if a product (such a pre made meal prep service) doesn't have a bar code you can spend 10 minutes searching for the meal to view every other meal in the database at least 4x. In less than a week this went from a potentially useful product for tracking macros to a constant headache and a great way to increase stress and encourge people to stop tracking

What happened to this app?? You can't do anything without paying for it. Adding a recipe manually, it changes everything you write. Then allows you to edit and still it's wrong. If you give up and want to just add the macros at least to have something, it only allows you to add calories, no carb, no protein, no anything. This used to be a good app now, NO WAY. UPDATE: Please if you intend to respond to reviews then at least respond with an actual response, not some stock reply with a link.

This app was perfect and I've gotten good and thorough use out of it! However, the new design is terrible! I liked being able to see everything laid out the way it was before... I shouldn't have to press a ton of buttons to get to see all the food I've eaten in a day. I should be able to quickly glance at my remaining calories and eaten food. I want the old interface back. At least give us a customization option to have the 'classic look.' Until then, one star. Bring it back & Ill change to 5.

OMG! With this latest update, the app is practically unusable! Can no longer edit a logged meal. When deleting multiple items, each must be done separately. Far more clicks to log and adjust a meal than ever. I have been a premium user for more than 6 years but I will be looking for a more practical solution than what MFP has become. Avoid this app! UPDATE: I can't believe all the steps now needed to look back at previous meals. WHAT WAS THE DEVELOPER THINKING!?

DO NOT USE WITH ANDROID!!! This isn't mentioned anywhere, but there is a major flaw that the app doesn't sync if you use with an android based phone. I logged for 8 months with my phone, only to lose 3/4 of my data when they did an update. Apparently, the developers are "aware" of this and working on it and they recommend NOT using the app, but rather the website instead. Completely ridiculous, unprofessional, and a terrible response.

for over a month the app and web version no longer sync. the problem is that all apps that use MFP data (like fitbit) pull from the web data. so now you have to enter everything on the web version which is a horrible UI and just not convenient. this is not a small temporary hiccup. it's been over a month!! their response? "we're working on it." they promise the next update will fix it. it doesn't. radio silence again.

Absolutely HATE the new update. I refuse to keep using it. the layout is not user friendly whatsoever and is not "simplified". there was absolutely no need to change the layout, but apparently they just wanted to completely destroy the app. my biggest grievance- it automatically is adding my exercise into my calories without showing that it added it to my calories.Im trying to LOSE weight, I am working out to be in a larger deficit not to eat more. RIP MFP

Lots of clutter, constant ads, and pushing to buy premium. Getting worse over time, more and more pop up stuff. I don't need you to pop up a screen congratulating me for using the app every day. update: moved to one star. somehow they made it even worse. navigating through your food diary is way clunkier, added more steps and doesn't show all the food unless you go to a separate screen. no easy way to flip between days and see your diary on previous days. slower and more button pushes needed.

I have a paid subscription. My food diary on the app isn't syncing with the server, so my last week of logging isn't actually in the system. I contacted support, and they told me it's a known bug with no current fix. They said to look out for an update in the store for a fix, but can't even confirm when that update will be. They told me to manually enter on the site, not using the features I'm paying for and to redo my entries. Also I get ads for their premium plan that I already have.

It's ability to find and estimate food is useful, but not always accurate. My biggest issue is that the app miscalculated the number of calories I should be eating, and has caused me to only maintain and not lose weight despite being set to lose 2lbs per week. I had to do additional research to learn this as I was having no luck. I have a new app now that seems correct. This one miscalculated by about 500 calories per day, which was an increase of roughly 30%.

I regret paying for a yearly subscription. The barcode scanner is painfully slow (5-10 seconds per scan), the database constantly fails to recognize foods, and you're forced to manually enter everything yourself. The photo AI is a gimmick—it guesses wrong or identifies totally different foods. Logging takes forever. Save yourself the frustration and try MyNetDiary, Cronometer, or Fitia instead. They're faster, more accurate, and cheaper (or free). Learn from my mistake.

This app was working fairly well until this afternoon. Then, I got automatically logged out. After logging back in, most of my food entries for the last 2 days were missing. Shortly after adding back in the foods I could remember, the foods in my diary multiplied, showing a ridiculous amount of calories at least double or triple what I consumed. Updating and restarting the app didn't fix the problem, so I uninstalled and reinstalled the app. After reinstalling the app, I am unable to log back in

Update so terrible that it has fewer features than the older version, with no plan to incorporate those features. The nutrients I used to be able to track very easily are now buried under two or three layers of menus. Digging them up every single time I go to eat something just makes me not want to eat or not log what I eat, and both are the exact opposite of what this app should be doing and used to do for me. Yesterday, it could do it. Today, customer service says it can't. Such a waste of $

I've been using this for several years on and off. in the last year I used it extensively to lose 90 pounds and achieve my goal weight, along with major health improvements. But what deranged lunatic came up with the recent update (3/3/2025)? This went from being intuitive and easy to use, to literally hiding the information you want behind to see. I can't see what I've logged without several clicks and the app fighting against me seeing my log. Stupid, and I'm probably deleting it!

I literally just downloaded this app this morning and I've already encountered problems! I actually liked using the app throughout the day and was going to log a snack, when poof! I signed up for the seven day trial and was going to purchase it, but now with this happening so soon, I'm not so sure.

The new update to the interface as of 3/3/26, is worse in every way. it is less intuitive and displays less information than the previous version of the app. Previous versions had substantially more information on the initial screen and was easily navigable. I have no idea why these changes were instituted. It seems to me that it has changed from a meal and macro record to a meal prep app. When my premium membership runs out, I will be moving to something else.

I used to use this app daily, but stopped (life happened) for a while. Now the app won't let me log in- I get the blue MFP screen with a spinning circle indefinitely. I can login to the webpage, but not the app, and it doesn't sync to Garmin Connect. I opened a ticket over a month ago, and they 'are working on it,' but they closed my ticket & it doesn't seem to be a priority. It's sad to see what happened to this app.

Installed app with trial. logged breakfast and lunch, but the app only chose to save the breakfast log. Even though I have a trial, the app will not acknowledge it. Play store says to log in with 3 days or it will be canceled. Plenty of other apps out there that do the same thing for free or less than $20/month. edit: ads take up 50% of the app on screen. complete waste of time/effort.

I used to love using MyFitnessPal, but now you have to pay for it?? I just redownloaded the app from when I used it to sound calories in 2021, and it has so many glitches! I put in bacon and it, at first said no matches, then when matches showed up, it would glitch and go back to the initial input screen. I'm not sure what is up with FREE apps, but they all want you to pay and they all have glitches and aren't as easy as they used to be to use! I'm outta here.👎👎👎

I liked it when it was working. Now it takes forever to load and sometimes doesn't load at all. Update 2/22/26: Between the price hikes,poor features, the app constantly logging me out,and now the app just not loading anymore, I'm no longer renewing my premium membership and moving on to something more reliable. The way greed ruins good products is sad.

I used to love this app. I downloaded it again today, and after installing, it opened once. It would not perform the logging function (clicked + and app just grayed out/nothing happened.) I tried this more than once, then tried closing and reopening the app. Now it won't get past the loading screen, and this is continuing to happen after a phone reboot. Very disappointed.

It's been 2 years since my last review & same exact problems 2 years later. The app has more food items already programmed by barcode compared to other apps. It can pair to several apps. It doesn't pair with Samsung Health! Only pairs with a specific glucose app. The app doesn't have body measurements with the premiun version. Can't change your username in the app. I don't want to go to the website to do things I should be able to do in app. MFP doesn't care.

upon opening the app I'm bombarded to join premium 3 times back to back. I exit out of the "offer" and it pops up again. Syncing to other apps is terrible. Searching for food to add to each meal and suddenly I'm "offline" but every other app works fine. Cool that it syncs with the Garmin app but it randomly logged me out and my password wouldn't work. It also changed my default log in email on me almost like it's mixing up 2 of my emails.

horrible update. everything is harder to do now. did you test this update at all? I have to click so many more times now to do anything edit: the more I use this new version, I keep finding new ways to hate it. I miss the very clear calculation of my base calories, my added calories from exercise, my consumed calories, and my remaining calories. this has all been "simplified" and made completely esoteric. it's awful

Genuinely crashes more than any other app I have ever used. This is a silly, poorly designed calorie counter... Why exactly does this crash more than any game I've ever tried? If I got a dolla for every time it failed, crashed, shut down, or did some other stupid glitch, I'd have anough money for a liftime membership... Just bad, try something else. Forget the UI change! Fix the app first! still crashes all the time!

Was good for tracking calories. Has a large bank updated by users. Has some good charts Integrations are unreliable (e.g. Strava import) Menus have become more hidden behind clicks over time e.g. Harder to find the menu on weight progress, Harder to get to your daily log, etc. The app keeps adding "improvements" that make it harder to use e.g. it's harder to customize water logging now. There are also some bugs with syncing exercises to the server. the new ui update makes it even worse :/

New dashboard/diary layout negatively impacted the way I interact with the App. Personally, I found it to be a detriment to how I use MyFitnessPal, and the lack of an option to stick with the previous layout is disappointing. Older versions were more succinct and fit my routine. This change will influence my decision to renew my membership, as I personally don't find value in the new layout or user experience (though others may disagree).

the new layout is not as helpful and intuitive as it was intended, the version prior to this one has everything for the day easily visible , had to do fewer clicks. best case add an option to choose layout, worst case; make it so all the foods are visible in the diary without clicking on a particular meal, I'd rather scroll down the list then click into something, next add the orange bar and the counter to calories remaining to show calories burned instead of just ticking the calorie limit

This app used to be AMAZING. I got so many people to download and start using it. I have been using it since 2017 and even with features moving to premium over the years, I still loved it but ever since the new update it's just terrible. This upgrade was the worst thing they could've done for the app. its not user friendly. deleted all saved foods, pop ups like crazy and makes it seem impossible to track anything. They just need to bring the old version back. This was a big mistake changing it.

The new UI is terrible. Calorie counting is the main thing I use this app for and I liked when it clearly displayed the calories from food, calories burned from exercise, and how many I had left before going over my calorie goal for each day. I cannot figure out how to get that kind of display of information back.

well it has gone down hill. used to add food and it would track it and tell you your nutritional values for free, it also used to let you add recipes ( like meat, water, salt. which I eat often so it is even less useful) I recently re-downloaded and it still lets me log in food but it will not tell me the national values, or add recipes anymore unless I pay.

3/14/2026 update: It got better for a while, but it's logged me out twice TODAY! The app keeps logging me out, which is a pain when I just want to add something to the food diary. And the constant requests to upgrade are getting very old. If I want to upgrade, I will, but the ads for it make me determined NOT to upgrade.

The premium service is good for tracking water/food intake, provide access to community/recipes. You can connect your fitness tracker to sync your activity with MFP. This feature d/n work. I have a Samsung, others complained of same issue with Fitbit. Troubleshoot: disconnect tracker, uninstall/reinstall MFP, reconnect tracker, sync. Repeat for each time you want to sync steps. NO TY! Sleep NEVER crosses over. Have to pay to use barcode scanner.

Updating my review. I have used MFP for well over 10 years, & have loved everything about it. I used it this morning (3/11/2026), & by this afternoon there was an update. It's now absolutely horrendous! Zero user friendly. There's plenty of free food tracking apps, & if this newest update is where MFP wants to go with this, I'll gladly leave for another one. Utterly disappointed. For anyone looking for a similar free food tracking app, check out MacrosFirst or Cronometer.

Hate the newest update. Way to make it way more time-consuming to log the foods you normally eat. 🙄 Edit: No bug, it's just the way it was designed. Foods get added to the totals and do not appear on the daily list of foods. This makes it so you can not delete/edit them food. Sometimes, they are added to the Meal total, sometimes not. Either way, your daily totals come out incorrect. This is enough to make me not want to use this app anymore. Not cool.

I decided not to subscribe after the free trial, mainly because it is quite buggy. There are so many syncing issues with fitbit I'm never sure what my actual allowance is. Sometimes it won't sync my exercise. Sometimes 1000s of phantom extra calories are added and disappear again later. Graphs don't always update when you change dates. I will keep trying with the free version for a bit and then decide if it is worth subscribing or instead trying a different app. Update. It's so buggy. I quit.

Probably the worst tracking I've ever ever used. I'd rather use excel than the app. 25% of the screen is adds and useless info. I use the site on a pc and it is much better. I try to go back and the app takes me out and closes it. My macro preferences doesn't stick. My calories are way too low but trying to figure it out on the app with such little screen space around the import stuff makes it impossible. I'm just going to use the PC since the app is such garbage.

They ruined this app. I have used this app on and off over the years and it used to be extremely user-friendly and the barcode scanner was free. Latest update makes it unusable unless you have a Premium account (which I can't afford and don't need extra bells and whistles). No longer able to scan a barcode without Premium, which means you can't get an accurate food and calories. Would give it zero if I could. Every other calorie logging app allows barcode scanning so this is a ripoff and bait and switch tactic (which is absolutely illegal so I'll be reporting you)!

The new update is both hideous and difficult to use. I'm seriously considering cancelling my subscription and trying out a different calorie counter. Trying to find anything on the app takes a lot longer than it used to, the recent history foods is so much shorter that I have to keep re-seatching for or scanning foods that I eat every other week. Not at all happy. If it's not reverted by the end of the month I'd be shocked, and I'd be out.

I have only been a app user for about 4 months and it was helpful to keep me accountable. It was also good for me to be able to have a visual reference. I joined after the bar code scanner was changed to premium so that was not a big deal, but now they have moved the Macros behind their PayWall. Disappointed is only one way to say it. Save the "We will share with the team" MFP and just do better.

All I wanted to do was change my email address from an old account to a new one, since I cannot access the old one reliably. The website gave me error messages that it was an invalid address multiple times. It is not invalid and there was no typo. I got into a chat with the AI and it kept suggesting steps I should try, and when I said I had already done so, it just kept looping around in the same 3 sets of recommendations. I deleted my account. No money for you. Bad/nonexistent customer service.

unfortunately having to downgrade my previous review after the latest updates since you took away/hid several useful features: 1. No longer able to move logged food to different meal/day 2. No longer able to copy a meal to a new day. 3. No longer able to bulk update logged time. 4. No longer able to bulk delete. and this is just what I've found so far. Nice new UI, but the best functionality went away.

I have been using this app for at least 7 years. I pay for premium so I can track my macros and it has been perfect for all this time. A couple of days ago I opened the app to find it completely changed. I can no longer see my entire day at a glance to figure out where I can make changes but have to click on each separate meal. I can no longer copy meals from the day before or create meals. So many other issues- it's more work! I may be searching for a new app at this point.

Have been using MFP for years and loved it. NOT ANYMORE. New UI that was just pushed is horrible. It's not remotely intuitive, options that were front and center now take multiple clicks to get to, it's just awful. I'm so angry I just upgraded and already paid for a year's subscription, cause this new version is absolutely NOT WORTH IT. And that really sucks, because I LOVED this app before. Definitely cancelling this subscription.

Update: I am wondering if there will there be an update anytime soon. I can't see paying $20/month when the last update has been so awful and time consuming to track. So many features that once were there are gone. So disappointed still. I hate the new update. I often eat the same foods and can't copy the meal to a different day like I could in the past. Makes tracking more cumbersome and takes longer.

The most recent update basically makes the app unusable! You can no longer swipe back and forth to navigate from one day to another. You cannot copy parts of a meal to another day. You cannot copy a meal from several days ago. Performance of the UI sluggish. Logging food take 2x as many operations to complete. Search no longer finds recently used food. Meal descriptions have lost value as "Eggs and 2 more" is pretty useless. The silver text on black background is hard to read.

I used to use this app to log my meals and see where I am missing certain nutrients a day (and where I need to adjust) for medical reasons. Now, the new UI change has made it difficult to do this quickly and efficiently. And since I have limited time in the day to do things, it has now become a hindrance. I liked the older UI. It made everything easier to do and see. Because of this new UI, I am now canceling my premium subscription and uninstalling the app.

I had used this app years ago and absolutely loved it. decided to come back and try it again to help me lose some weight that I recently gained back. upsetting that I cannot use intermittent fasting tracker, barcode to add food quickly, or add the time of day that I ate something because it's all a premium price. I'm going to try this for a few more days, but most likely we'll be deleting it. very upsetting

I've used this app for many years and this newest update is so bad. Trying to scroll back to previous dates is no longer quick. SO MANY extra taps to add one food item or to view my entire day. Entire day or meals; not both. I keep selecting PLAN at the bottom, thinking it will expand my day so I can see what I've added, but it's a digital planner, which is a premium PLUS service. I already pay for Premium. I don't want the plus, AND I can't remove it from the main task bar. No renewal for me.

I downloaded this app in Aug (its the middle of Oct) and I have loved it for the past 2 months other than a little bug with my samsung health and MFP talking to each other its been great! It takes 4, 5, 6 times as long to find things that were readily available right on the dashboard before this catastrophe. Go onto Reddit and read for yourself.

I used to love MyFitnessPal but with the recent updates, it is practically unusable without paying for the subscription. You can no longer scan barcodes (a feature that used to be free) and oftentimes, even when searching for exact names of products, it will not pull them up. It seems the app has intentionally made it so difficult and time consuming to add each food manually to push you towards paying the subscription. This app used to be great, but has become so bloated with premium offers, ads, and paywalled features that it's practically useless unless you want to shell out for another subscription service. Lost key features in the app. Last app version everything was great. Been using this app for about 4 months now, and I found it great for tracking macros and keeping track of my calorie intake. This used to be a good app. I don't like that I can't manually enter foods, I have to choose from their list which shows items having more calories than they actually do. I wish I could give this app a -1 star! I have been a premium subscriber for many years, however they decided to add a premium+ subscription which supersedes the premium (paid) version. Was a great app until the latest update. I absolutely hate the latest update to this app. I've been using the paid version for several years, fairly happily. Now I pretty much hate everything about it. It's hard to navigate. The daily calorie goal set by the app is way above maintenance for days where I'm less active. Sadly, there's no way to vary this number to coincide with my training schedule.

The app is completely unusable without paying. You literally cant do anything. Barcode scan? Quick add? All paid. It won't even show you your macros unless you pay. Using this app is worse than just creating a spreadsheet and tracking yourself. I used to pay. but I refuse to support such disgusting levels of greed. There are other fitness apps out there that provide way more features for free, and also cost less if you want to pay. Stay away from this app.

This app won't let you set your own daily caloric goal. Instead it sets a random goal and makes you adjust percentages of macros which don't actually lower the overall caloric goal. In trying to lose weight and it's telling me to eat 3000+ calories. Support is no help either, just saying to wait for a fix in the newest update but I've been waiting 6 months for one

You took what was not broke and found every way possible to make it worse. The new UI is awful. I can't copy meals anymore. All the charts are gone. The least you could have done is allowed us the option of what data we wanted presented and how we wanted to see it instead of just taking it all away and neutering the options for meal input. Please undo the damage you did and then put yourself in timeout until you feel adequately shamed and chastised.

Pay wall access for basic use. Most recent update blocks macros of complete meal(breakfast, lunch, dinner, and snacks). Greed at its best. Snyc and connectivity between complimenting apps is worthless and unreliable. No thanks to troubleshooting it's not me, it's you and your app! I would give this app a much higher rating if the meal macros were unlocked like they were before the latest update.

Oct 2025 android update has ruined the app, it's no longer user friendly and many of the features I loved have disappeared. Can no longer copy meals, can't easily move foods from one meal to another. You also have to click 2 more times to add a food and to go back to previous days. So perplexed and annoyed by this release. Frustrating that I already paid for a year of premium!

You had a great app but now it sucks. I see a lot of users agree with me and you guys apologise but don't seem to take actio to address all the ussues. I've loved using this app for more than 10 years until now. The recent update has taken away functionality that I use regularly. I can no longer save a meal that I have regularly. I can reuse yesterday's meal but not the meal from 2+ days ago. The meal planning function allows for little flexibility. I'm very unhappy with this new update.

It's terrible on my S25 Ultra. Buggy, search is complete garbage. I've used mfp for years since back when barcode scanning was available in free version and I lost some POUNDS. Recommended to my friends and fam. It's awful now. When I got the new phone, it would NOT recognize my old account info...also refused to let me create an account with that email bc it was in use. I uninstalled/reinstalled. No good. And now search is NOT working. Simple things like glazed donut come up with no results.

I can no longer recommend this app with the latest UI update. Logging is more confusing, and information that used to be easily available is now hidden away. Everything loads slower, and they introduced a button at the bottom whose only purpose is to display an ad for a subscription upsell. The app is now significantly less pleasing and more difficult to use for the simple tasks of logging food and viewing the foods you ate today and in the past week.

Another case of if it ain't broke dont fix it. New UI is atrocious. Log confuses you with suggested prior foods for a meal if you havent logged anything yet.. To see what you ate for a meal you have to actually click into that meal now and you can only see one meal at a time. I just want to see the whole day with a category for each meal. You cant see calculations for adjustments on the log either without doing mental math and assuming you remember your base daily goal. Nobody asked for this.

I HATE the new layout! I can't see everything I've eaten for a meal unless I click on it and it takes an extra click to add *each additional item* to a meal! Why add extra steps‽ Why does it take fiddling around for upwards of 30 seconds to get the barcodes to scan? The only value add to the updated layout was adding a quick click "log yesterday's meal again." I've been using this app for years, most of the updates have been very beneficial, but not this one!

Came back to this app after 5 years and everything is behind a paywall now. Features and functionalities that used to be free are now $80 per year. No thanks, if I can't scan a barcode and have to manually type in each food item, I'd rather go to a brand new app and just calculate and enter the calories myself. Ridiculous.

there was an update about a week ago. After the update completed, the app wanted me to log back in. For whatever reason the app refused to log me back until this latest update came out. I ended up losing a lot of progress. I no longer have any confidence in using this app and plan on looking elsewhere for my fitness goals. Edited: Just edited to say that I canceled my subscription.

The phone app and the online version rarely sync. I find the phone app barely usable and does me no good when I'm on the road. A week later and it doesn't sync any foods that I log daily. in fact I changed my calorie goals a month ago and it doesn't recognize that change. computer version works fine and I love it. If it doesn't work for me on the road/phone there is no reason to upgrade to the premium version.

Edit: The new update has ruined the seamless synergy of this app. I can't copy meals to a different day, can't save meals for easy logging in, and whenever you try to put your food info in you get exited out of the screen and have to jump through 3-4 more screens just add another item. Please revert these changes, this really makes the app annoying to use. All the new features really make this much easier to track my meals and diet.

Don't try to depend on any of their features working. Don't think for a minute that something they fix won't break with their next update. They don't belive in testing at all. An example is Reminders. As many times as they've broken them, you'd think that might be something they test for. Nah. Testing is for sissies, I guess. Don't know what that means about any sensitive data you might put in.

Just noticed yesterday I was charged for a premium subscription on Dec 6 without my knowledge. That's some deceptive business friends. I never received a notice about the charge either. It doesn't matter how good the app "might be" to me now, every bite I may log leaves a foul taste behind. AND YES, I requested a refund from Play.

I used this app for a long time tracking my meals every day. I loved it. Unfortunately, due to their UI update in October 2025, the app is no longer useful to me. The daily meal tracking screen (Today screen) doesn't allow for viewing all the day's meal entries in one scrollable view like it used to. It is now required to click into each meal grouping individually, and the Today screen is just cluttered with useless text. This also adds 2-3 taps to add food entries. Very disappointing. 😭

Note I had given this app 5 stars when I first reviewed it in February 2014. The most recent update has been a disaster. Contacted support - crickets; no response/reply; not even an acknowledgement! I can no longer copy a meal from one day to the next; the auto-complete search needs a solid 3-4 seconds after I type to search my history. I'm a premium member too - and if it doesn't get fixed quickly will be a former member.

very glitchy app. I have trouble signing in. the dashboard is blank if I go to diary and then want to return to dashboard. It won't let me connect my fitbit app as it says an account is already connected when it isn't. I went on fitbit and disconnected every app connection it had. still won't allow me to connect. I won't even entertain paying for it while it is this glitchy.

This used to be my go-to app. I haven't used it in years and am focusing on my weight loss goals again so I downloaded it again. You now have to pay to scan a bar code?? Can't believe they took away that feature from the free version, and I'm not paying $20 a month for an app. If you're going to make your app premium, don't TAKE AWAY features that were once free - that's how you upset your userbase.

Every single time. When you update your app you don't properly beta test it to see errors and fix them. What now? Well the user loses streaks displayed on their phone UNLESS you log a meal on your phone. Phone in the shop, log meals on your computer, streak gone. Absolutely pathetic. We are actively looking for a different service to log our meals. Steaks are what keep many people engaged in their weight loss journey and somehow you have f'd that up. Great Job!

Taking away a feature is the biggest no-no for me, whether it's apps, cell phones, whatever. This is a shameful practice. MyFitnessPal was my favorite app, but now they hide barcode scanning behind a paywall. I am using a different app now. Add new features that make me want to pay, don't take away formerly free ones.

Unstable and unreliable. Constantly releasing updates that break things. Sometimes it's food logging that breaks. Sometimes you can't login. Sometimes your weight logging is inverted and not within the correct range. It's a mess. Use a different app. It's been like this for as long as I can remember. To be clear : this is not about recent issues. MyFitnessPal has a history of releasing updates that break existing BASIC functionality all the time.

I used to use this app all the time. had great features and was perfect for tracking calories and macros. like others have stated, everything is behind a paywall now. I'm ok with ads to make up for it being free, but simple things like the barcode scanning is not available for free users. in the age of AI, I'll probably just build my own app to do this.

Downloaded this app thinking it tracked steps but it only tracks food intake. Wasn't what I wanted. Plus, there was supposed to be a free trial. Uninstalled it right away. Was charged for a full year! Even if it had the ability to track steps, it's definitely not worth the $59.99 they charge you for...not to mention NO FREE TRIAL!! False advertising!

Thanks to recent issues with the app, followed by the most aggravating, time consuming customer service experience in recent times (2 weeks, zero accomplished) ...I was finally able to cancel my Premium subscription (6 years) I had used it to log my food and stay focused and drop 50 lbs. Then,off and on to maintain. Needless to say, I've since stumbled onto an app that is so much better, for my usage, (tracking net carbs) than MFP ever had been. Quicker & easier to accurately log everything.

Ads and greed killed a good app. This used to be an amazing app years ago. I could scan my food, I could track my fitness from calories to exercise to water and even more! It was a great one-stop shop for all I needed fitness wise. The ads I expected early, as a free app. But over time, features that were free suddenly were paywalled by a subscription, like scanning foods. The app removed features, added more ads, and charged more for their subscriptions. I deleted my account and left.

New redesign for Android that dropped today is AWFUL. Now it takes more steps to log food, more steps to see what I ate in each meal, harder to find specific nutrient values per meal, and I'm unable to move foods around to a different meal. I wish I hadn't just re-upped my annual subscription, or I would cancel immediately. Why would you remove so much of the functionality that the previous version had? PLEASE change it back.

I've been using this app for years, and I have no problems with adding exercises to the diary until yesterday. Now when I click add exercise, the options for the type of exercise come up, but when I select any of them, the exercise window just disappears. Hard to track calories in and calories out if I can't input exercises. Please fix.

It's a nice app but it's glitchy and the database is truly awful. I find I'm having to manually enter the correct calories and macros for about 95% of my foods because what's in the database is wrong. A lot of the time you also can't select the correct portion sizes. The barcode scanner can sometimes take ages to focus and it's quicker to enter the data manually.

Absolutely horrendous app on Android. I can never use my previously logged food when logging something new in my diary unless I manually change a setting every time I log. as suggested on the website, I reinstalled the app to see if it fixes the issue, but it erased almost all my history. weird thing is that it didn't lose everything - just a few bits and pieces from every meal, which is even more odd. There is no easy way to contact support either and super disappointed with the user experience

Used this app for years to track my weight and food intake, I came back to it again recently to start being more serious about tracking my food again and they have taken away the scan bar code on the free version and the yearly subscription is a rip off just so I can scan bar codes. I'm going over to the Crono app instead as they have scan and are much cheaper. Sad that these apps are just turning into money grabbers all of a sudden when we are in a cost of living crisis.
